title: AI Portfolio Analyst
description: ICON plc is a world\-leading healthcare intelligence and clinical research organization. We’re proud to foster an inclusive environment driving innovation and excellence, and we welcome you to join us on our mission to shape the future of clinical development. As an **AI Portfolio Analyst** at ICON, you will play a key role in supporting the delivery, governance, and optimisation of the new Enterprise AI department. You will work at the intersection of business needs, AI capabilities, and delivery execution—ensuring that AI initiatives are aligned to strategic priorities, clearly defined, and effectively managed across their lifecycle. **What You Will Do** In this role you will be responsible for supporting the planning, analysis, and ongoing management of AI programmes and initiatives across the Enterprise AI function. **Key Responsibilities Include** * Partner with stakeholders to gather, define, and document business requirements for AI use cases, products, and platform capabilities * Support the intake, prioritisation, and tracking of AI initiatives within the Enterprise AI portfolio * Translate business needs into clear functional and non\-functional requirements, user stories, and acceptance criteria * Analyse processes and identify opportunities where AI and automation can deliver measurable value * Develop and maintain portfolio reporting, dashboards, and performance metrics to support decision\-making * Collaborate with engineering, product, and governance teams to ensure alignment between business requirements and technical delivery * Support governance processes, including steering committees, portfolio reviews, and status reporting * Identify risks, dependencies, and blockers across AI programmes, and support mitigation planning * Contribute to continuous improvement of portfolio management, business analysis practices, and AI delivery frameworks **Your Profile** To succeed in this role, you will bring strong analytical and business analysis experience, along with an interest in emerging AI technologies and their application in regulated environments. **Required Qualifications And Experience** * Bachelor’s degree in Business, Information Systems, Life Sciences, or a related discipline * Proven experience as a Business Analyst, Portfolio Analyst, or similar role within technology or data\-driven environments * Experience gathering and documenting requirements and translating them into deliverable outcomes * Strong understanding of project and portfolio management principles * Familiarity with AI/ML concepts and digital transformation initiatives * Excellent analytical skills with the ability to interpret data and produce actionable insights * Strong communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to work across technical and non\-technical teams * Ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver in a fast\-paced, evolving environment **What ICON Can Offer You** Our success depends on the quality of our people. That’s why we’ve made it a priority to build a diverse culture that rewards high performance and nurtures talent. In addition to your competitive salary, ICON offers a range of additional benefits. Our benefits are designed to be competitive within each country and are focused on well\-being and work life balance opportunities for you and your family. **Our Benefits Examples Include** * Various annual leave entitlements * A range of health insurance offerings to suit you and your family’s needs. * Competitive retirement planning offerings to maximize savings and plan with confidence for the years ahead. * Global Employee Assistance Programme, LifeWorks, offering 24\-hour access to a global network of over 80,000 independent specialized professionals who are there to support you and your family’s well\-being. * Life assurance * Flexible country\-specific optional benefits, including childcare vouchers, bike purchase schemes, discounted gym memberships, subsidized travel passes, health assessments, among others. Visit our careers site to read more about the benefits ICON offers. At ICON, inclusion \& belonging are fundamental to our culture and values. We’re dedicated to providing an inclusive and accessible environment for all candidates. ICON is committed to providing a workplace free of discrimination and harassment. All qualified applicants will receive equal consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or protected veteran status. If, because of a medical condition or disability, you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the application process, or in order to perform the essential functions of a position, please let us know or submit a request here Interested in the role, but unsure if you meet all of the requirements? We would encourage you to apply regardless – there’s every chance you’re exactly what we’re looking for here at ICON whether it is for this or other roles. Are you a current ICON Employee? Please click here to apply

title: AIT LAB Systems Engineer

title: ASSISTANT FINANCIAL CONTROLLER

activities * Support onsite logistics including material handling, stock control, equipment checks and management of consumables **Skills and experience:** * Excellent customer service and diagrams * Experience working on construction sites and coordinating AV installations alongside main contractors and other trades * Knowledge of structured cabling, containment systems and AV installation practices * Willingness to travel nationally and internationally when required * Experience working with new and emerging technologies **If you are selected for interview, and need any title: Accounts Admin - Consultant Paperwork, Queries & OP Claims
description: **Job Title: Accounts Admin \- Consultant Paperwork, Queries \& OP Claims** **Location: Blackrock Clinic, Rock Road, Blackrock, A94 E4X7** **Department: Finance** **Job Type: Permanent** **Hours: 37 hours per week (Full\-Time)** Blackrock Clinic is the leading and longest established private hospital and clinic in Ireland. Since it opened in the mid\-1980s, the clinic has consistently built an unparalleled reputation in new high\-tech surgical procedures, medical treatments, and ground\-breaking diagnostics. Blackrock Clinic is now part of Blackrock Health private hospital group along with the Hermitage Clinic, Galway Clinic, and Limerick Clinic, some of the most advanced hospitals in Irish private healthcare. As a JCI accredited hospital, Blackrock Clinic is a progressive and pioneering hospital in which care for the patient is central to everything we do. **Role Purpose:** * Maintain Mediclaim to ensure consultant name data is cleansed, corrected if necessary and kept accurate on a daily basis and that claims in each consultant list are in line with their speciality, so we are chasing paperwork from the correct person * Action queries on a daily basis and update Mediclaim to reflect the resolution for example if a consultant has transferred care or if we have sufficient paperwork already in plac * Produce reports from Mediclaim in excel for billing agents and self\-billing consultants to chase outstanding paperwork due for claim submission * Manage the accounts shared mailbox, action queries and requests and scan up paperwork emailed in from self\-billing consultants * Ensure all Patient claims are collated correctly and efficiently to maintain regular and even submission volumes and targets * Maintain a high standard of quality checking thus minimising the number of pended, returned or rejected claims * Deal with Patients, Consultant and Staff queries in an efficient, professional and courteous manner at all times. * Being personally accountable for your own actions at all times **Skills / Experience:** * Proficient in Microsoft Excel * Excellent communication skills both verbal \& written **Key Responsibilities:** * Review newly dropped claims in Mediclaim to ensure the consultant names are accurate, the patient claim form has been collected at registration, and the hospital invoice is within SLA * Manage the missing side 1 worklist in Mediclaim * Produce reports in excel of outstanding paperwork from Mediclaim, control checking against our financial reports to ensure the volumes match, analysing accuracy and aging and applying different methods of paperwork retrieval best suited to the consultant at hand * Locate charts and retrieve pertinent forms to use in the chasing of paperwork * Review Patient claim packs for accuracy and completeness and obtain any missing information required in order to submit the claim for assessment to the relevant Insurer * Identification of claims with errors that cannot be submitted to the Insurers and being pro\-active in trying to resolve this in a timely manner * Daily scanning of paperwork received outside of Mediclaim into the system * Assessment and submission of outpatient scans on a daily basis * Adhere to and a fully understand of the Insurer contracts with which in the claims checking process * Weekly reconciliation of claims submitted to each Insurer and filing of claim submissions * Deal with routine calls from patients, consultants and staff. Queries must be resolved daily or escalated to the Patient Accounts Manager if further action is required. A professional and courteous approach is expected at all times, for both internal and external customers * Supporting the standardisation and improvement of processes, procedures, controls and systems * Utilisation of Excel and Word Processing skills to maintain up\-to\-date spreadsheets and statistics * Carry out other such duties as required by the Patient Accounts Manager **Communication Skills:** * Communicate appropriately with colleagues and other members of the health care teams to ensure a quality service is provided to all while constantly striving for excellence in patient/customer care. * Communicate in a manner that avoids antagonism, reduces conflict and prevents undue anxiety. * Recognise limitations and seek assistance where necessary. * Attend and contribute to appropriate meetings. * Accept constructive advice when appropriate. * Report all incidents immediately, which may adversely affect a patient, colleague or the Blackrock Clinic. **General Expectations:** * Support the philosophy, objectives and goals of Blackrock Clinic. * Observe the appropriate lines of authority. * Maintain strict confidentiality regarding all patients and the activities of Blackrock Clinic. * Demonstrate flexibility by assisting in all areas of the hospital/clinic as required. * The Ward/Unit Manager depending on the availability of locum cover will arrange annual leave; this usually means the holiday periods i.e. Easter, summer and Christmas. * Participate in developing and providing quality care within Blackrock Clinic. * Assist in orientation of new personnel. * Continue to develop personal knowledge and skills and seeks outside educational opportunities. * Present a professional appearance and conform to the dress, hygiene and infection control policies of the Clinic. * Is always punctual on duty. * Flexible attitude to changing work practises. **Why work at the Blackrock Clinic:** At the Blackrock Clinic, we are passionate about our people and believe in their development and growth. As a member of the team, you can benefit from: * Competitive salary * Pension * Discounted cafe * Sports and Social club * Employee Assistance Programme * Discounted onsite pharmacy **Please Note:** ***Job descriptions are a reflection of the present service requirements and may be subject to review and amendments to meet the changing needs of the service. Please note that we reserve the right to close this vacancy early if sufficient applications are received.*** ***Blackrock Clinic is an Equal Opportunities employer, and we are committed to creating an environment that promotes equality and dignity at work.*** ***Blackrock Clinic is committed to providing reasonable accommodation when needed. Please advise if you have particular requirements, so that we can look into arranging reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process. You can contact us at HR@blackrockhealth.com*** ***It is essential that you have valid work permission for the Republic of Ireland to take up employment for this position, or in the case of critical skills, you must be eligible for a work permit.***

title: Activity Co Ordinator - Full time / Part time position
description: **Job Summary** To coordinate provision of a broad range of meaningful \& individualised activities that promote the physical \& mental health and wellbeing of the resident and provide opportunities for residents to socialise both within the home and with the wider community. This is achieved through the planning and evaluation of individual and group programmes of care, which are based on needs, identified where possible in consultation with the residents and delivered through day\-to\-day shared life experiences. The personal attributes which are necessary include creativity, person centredness, reliability and trustworthiness, empathy, compassion, and maturity. The candidate must be open\-minded, demonstrate resilience and a commitment to consistently delivering positive outcomes for all residents in line with their general ability. The activity coordinator will typically co\-ordinate staff allocated to activities both for groups and individuals. This person will liaise with third parties for the delivery of in\-house and external entertainment and meaningful activity. They must also be able to provide direct person\-to\-person meaningful activity individually and in groups. The activity coordinator will seek to provide a caring, stable environment in which various social, educational and relationship interventions can take place in the day\-to\-day living space of the residents, in a way that allows the residents to flourish and feel part of a caring community. Previous experience in a similar position would be an advantage. description: Role \- Part Time Administrator Hours \- Monday \- Friday 10am \- 14\.00pm Location \- Dublin 2 Strata Financial is one of Ireland's top 20 leading accounting practices, offering a comprehensive range of services, including accounting, auditing, tax, management, compliance, and restructuring. We work with clients across diverse industries, such as music, hospitality, SMEs, property, and franchising. We are currently expanding our administrative team and are hiring an experienced administrator to join us on a part time basis \- 20 hours per week. **Reception Cover** * Cover lunch break and ad hoc absences. Must be fully up to speed in reception duties if longer cover required. **Daily Tasks** * Morning check across three floors: * Cleaning standards * Kitchen supplies (milk, tea/coffee) * Boardroom fridges stocked * Stationery organised and replenished * Put away deliveries * Distribute post * Respond to ad hoc requests / issues * Light IT troubleshooting (where possible) **Weekly Tasks** * Full stock take (kitchen, boardrooms, cleaning, stationery, tech supplies) * Distribute drinks and snacks across floors **Ongoing Responsibilities** * Meeting room setup on request * Manage outgoing post and couriers * Maintenance coordination (escort contractors as needed) * Asset management (loan devices, repairs, tracking) * New starter desk setup and onboarding support * Filing (on\-site and off\-site) * Staff communications (bulletins as required) * Assist PM in small tasks as needed

title: Administrator (Advanced Excel)
description: We are currently seeking a highly organised and proactive **Administrative Support Executive/Analyst** to join a busy State Services team. This is an excellent opportunity for someone with strong administrative and customer service experience who is keen to develop their career within a structured team environment. **Salary:** €32,000\-€36,000 DOE \+ bonus **Location:** Dublin 18 **The Role** This role provides comprehensive administrative support within a team structure and involves daily interaction with internal and external stakeholders via phone and email. You will work closely with established processes and procedures to ensure high service levels, accuracy, and timely delivery across day\-to\-day operations and project\-related activities. You will play a key role in keeping systems up to date, managing scheduled events, supporting fleet\-related activities, and ensuring tasks are completed ahead of deadlines. Over time, there is strong scope to grow, learn, and progress within the wider team. **Key Responsibilities** * Provide day\-to\-day administrative support within the State Services team * Manage phone and email enquiries through shared team inboxes * Proactively manage daily, weekly, and monthly scheduled tasks * Maintain accurate system updates including data entry and reporting * Support fleet\-related administration such as fuel cards, toll tags, vehicle collections, hire cars, fines, road tax, GPS, and pool vehicles * Coordinate CVRT administration with internal teams and external suppliers * Assist with vehicle ordering, deliveries, and logistics planning * Liaise with other departments to resolve queries efficiently * Attend team meetings, recording minutes and tracking actions * Ensure all procedures, timelines, and service standards are adhered to * Contribute to team goals and individual performance targets **About You** * Minimum **1 year’s experience** in an administrative role * Excellent verbal and written communication skills * Strong customer service mindset with a friendly and professional approach * High attention to detail and accuracy * Confident user of **Microsoft Excel, Word, PowerPoint, and Outlook \- especially strong excel skills** * Organised, adaptable, and able to manage fluctuating workloads * A collaborative team player who enjoys contributing to shared success * Calm under pressure with a positive, “can\-do” attitude * Comfortable taking initiative and seeing tasks through to completion *By applying to this job, your CV will be assessed by a consultant from Recruiters.ie for the position described and your CV may be added to our secure applicant tracking system however your details will never be shared to any other third party without your consent.*

title: Administrator (Part-Time)
description: **About The Role** **Role responsibilities:** * Generate purchase orders for stock and non\-stock orders. * Processing purchase invoices matching them against goods received notes and purchase orders. * Preparation of month end supplier payment run file and logging of other one\-off payments on internet banking. * Month end supplier statement reconciliations to creditors ledger and dealing with supplier queries. * Processing changes to supplier banking details. * Inventory control to include placing stock orders in conjunction with General manager \& Operations managers, processing stock received/stock issued \& participation in quarterly stock counts. * Responding to other ad hoc requests from the business for financial assistance. * Company administration (insurance/tolls/vehicle management etc.) * Management of office area including telephone answering duties, point of contact to visitors, general administration duties and PA support to general manager when required. * HR admin – keeping employee records up to date with including holidays/sickness/training etc. * Keep H \& S policies updates and liaise with general manager to ensure records are up to date. * Process weekly oil samples for shipping. * Keep records of all issues personal PPE equipment (gas monitors/mobile phones/PPE) and arrange servicing/replacement as necessary. * Keep/maintain all records for Outside contractors (insurance/RAMS etc) **Key Skills** * Minimum 2–3 years’ experience in an administrative environment * Proficient in Microsoft Excel, Word, and PowerPoint * Strong attention to detail with a proven ability to meet deadlines * Ability to work independently and as part of a team * Willingness to support and cover across different departments as needed * Excellent organisational skills * Strong verbal and written communication skills **Working Hours \& Schedule** * Part\-time role (approximately 4 hours per day over 5 days per week) * Monday to Friday * Flexible working hours (morning or afternoon shifts available) **About You** About Us **We are Panda. We value waste.** We’re in the business of waste management but that doesn’t just mean collecting bins. Behind every collection is a passionate team of people finding smarter, safer, and more sustainable ways to recycle and repurpose materials that would otherwise go to waste. Over the past 30 years, Panda has grown into a leading resource recovery business, bringing together almost 3,000 people across multiple brands, united by a shared vision and strong values. While our operations are diverse, our purpose is clear: to protect our people, support our customers, and create positive impact for the communities and environments we serve. At Panda, **people are at the heart of everything we do** . We work as one team, combining different skills, experiences, and perspectives to achieve shared goals. We care for each other’s wellbeing, encourage development, and support growth because we know we’re stronger together. We expect **excellence** in how we operate. High standards, bold thinking, and continuous improvement drive our passion for innovation. We challenge the ordinary, put safety first, and embrace new ideas while delivering practical, cost\-effective solutions that move our business forward. We **own what we do** . From day\-to\-day tasks to major projects, we take pride in our work and responsibility for the outcome. We don’t walk past problems we address them together. Our expertise, reliability, and care reflect who we are and the difference we make. And above all, we act with **trust and integrity** . Trust is built through open communication, honesty, and accountability. It allows us to move quickly, make confident decisions, and deliver results — knowing our people are empowered to do the right thing. Our journey depends on talented, committed people who want to make an impact. **Take the first step and join us on the journey.** *Panda is committed to attracting and retaining a skilled and diverse workforce that reflects the communities in which we operate and the people we serve. (DE\&I Policy Statement)* Apply Share * Share Vacancy on Facebook * Share Vacancy on X * Share Vacancy on LinkedIn * Share Vacancy on WhatsApp * Send Vacancy to a friend

title: Apple Service Technician
description: **Service Technician** **Dublin HQ in Fonthill, Dublin 22** **Full\-time** **Competitive Salary €30,900 \- €32,000 (DOE) plus performance management commission** **Have you ever wondered what is inside an iPhone or a MacBook and how they work?** Select (part of the Select Technology Group) is the leading Apple Premium Reseller in the UK and Ireland. What do you get from working at Select? * A growing love and passion for all things Apple * Competitive salary * Fantastic Employee discounts * Employee Incentives * Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) * Recognition of Long Service * Pension Scheme (Subject to eligibility) We are looking for a dynamic **full\-time** Service Technician in our Service Centre in Dublin HQ. Showcasing the full Apple product line in a customer\-friendly environment, Select is renowned for providing outstanding customer service. Whether you are a consumer or professional customer, you will enjoy friendly, knowledgeable advice and support at a store conveniently located near you. The training you will receive will give you the enviable position of being the first to hear about new Apple products and initiatives. Above all else you are there to ensure that every customer receives the best experience of shopping with Select. This position has two main objectives: * The primary objective is to offer a high level of service and support to our customers, ensuring your location has all the resources required to consistently offer the best repair service. * The secondary objective is to offer a high level of service and support to our business and education customers while maintaining good relations with the internal business team. **Job Summary** As a Service Technician you will be responsible for the overall performance of your location with regards to service and repairs. You will be responsible for establishing a consistent, efficient, and high performing location and maintaining the high standards required. Your location will provide our customers with the best impression of our business, attaining where possible the required metrics set out by Apple. You will undertake all aspects of service\-related duties carried out in the location including repair ingestion, physical repair of all apple devices, inventory management, customer relations and control over Apple metric scores. **Responsibilities and Duties** **These include but are not limited to:** Ingestion of service items to location Updating Customers on the progress of their repair over the phone and via email Managing Customer expectations Using service XML and service portal Promoting Select services Troubleshooting Hardware and Software including diagnosis of faults Using relevant Apple Technologies to input data Ordering replacement parts, accessories and devices via GSX and/or Service portal Fitting Apple parts \& performing quality checks and inspections on devices Generating quotes and invoices for customers Keeping up to date with Apple and Select's training programmes Committing to each individual repair and doing your very best as an Apple Certified Technician Updating GSX appointment scheduling in conjunction with the Store Manager Train new members of the team on all aspects of service **Skills \& Competencies Required** Knowledge of best practice for service. Ability to maintain effective communication and working relationships with all touch points. Strong organisational skills with a core focus on attention to detail. Patience and an ability to work under pressure and time restraints. Excellent customer service skills. A keen interest in technology and a desire to keep up to date with the newest products **Preferred Additional Skills** Knowledge of/a good understanding of Consumer Rights Understanding of "Service" and hold current ACiT/ACMT/ACSP certifications If this sounds like a place you would like to work, you are aged 18 years or over, have the Right To Work in Ireland and you can demonstrate your passion, knowledge and determination, then we would like to hear from you. Please note that this is a full\-time role. To apply for the role of Service Technician, please submit your CV. **IND1**

title: Application Support Analyst
description: **Overview** As a Trading Application Support Analyst, you will join the post trade support team, providing technical and administrative support for all trading, operational, and support systems within the team. You will collaborate with development teams, Operations, and other key business stakeholders. This demanding and technical role requires strong technical systems and application knowledge to make critical support decisions under pressure. You will also manage complex project work for new or existing support systems, monitoring systems, support tools, and the deployment of new software trading systems. **Responsibilities** * Analyse and diagnose issues raised by business users related to trade data or system configurations. * Maintain and manage the batch scheduling environment and monitoring systems. * Remotely manage and monitor server hosts in multiple colocations across Europe. * Manage and prioritise requests from internal IT support functions. * Set up and maintain tools that monitor for alerts and test new system changes. * Continuously evaluate, improve, and deploy new application versions. * Serve as a contact point and collaborate with other infrastructure and software teams to ensure scalability, and stability. * Manage project work, including hardware refreshes, environment optimisation, software configurations, change management, and release management. **What We're Looking For** * + Excellent IT analytical and troubleshooting skills, with extensive experience tracing problems through log file analysis and understanding application/systems logic. + Experience with or supporting a batch scheduling system is an advantage. + Experience with application monitoring and analytical tools such as Splunk and Check\_mk. + SQL, DevOps and automation experience. + Experience in finance is a significant plus. + A B.S. in a technical discipline or equivalent experience. Knowledge of or a desire to develop networked application systems, including exposure to database environments and Windows (2012, 2019\). **Personal Skills** * Initiative: Proven record of seeking continuous improvement and implementing best practices in a technology environment. * Ability to work under pressure and meet tight deadlines. * Self\-motivated and creative. * Strong communication skills (verbal and written): Ability to convey technically complex subjects clearly and concisely. * Attention to detail: Thorough in work carried out. * Interpersonal skills: Friendly and approachable. * Teamwork: Ability to work collaboratively to meet deadlines. * Excellent time management, organisation, and prioritisation skills. * Flexibility: Desire to learn and improve skills and knowledge. Desire to drive business impact. *If you're a recruiting agency and want to partner with us, please reach out to* *recruiting@sig.com* *. Any resume or referral submitted in the absence of a signed agreement will not be eligible for an agency fee.*

title: Applied Data Scientist
description: Data Scientist (AI/ML) — Mid\-level (3–5 years) ABOUT THE ROLE We build agentic AI applications for large\-scale enterprise clients, including network support systems, recommendation engines, etc.. You'll work at the intersection of applied machine learning and production software — fine\-tuning LLMs, routing between agents, and building retrieval pipelines that solve real operational problems. This is a hands\-on engineering role, not a research position. You will own solutions end\-to\-end, from prototype to production. RESPONSIBILITIES\- Design, fine\-tune, and evaluate AI solutions for domain\-specific tasks (network support, ops automation)\- Build and maintain LLM routing logic — selecting and orchestrating between multiple model providers based on task, cost, and latency requirements\- Integrate LLMs into agentic AI workflows and orchestration layers\- Implement RAG pipelines including chunking strategies, embedding models, and vector store management when retrieval is required\- Write clean, production\-ready Python code following modern project tooling standards (Hatch, pyproject.toml)\- Collaborate with engineering, product, and client stakeholders to translate business requirements into data and model solutions\- Evaluate model performance using rigorous metrics; run A/B tests and regression tests on model changes\- Monitor deployed models in production and respond to drift, degradation, or failure modes\- Document technical decisions and contribute to internal knowledge sharing REQUIRED SKILLS Languages \& tooling: Python, Hatch, SQL,GitLLM \& AI: Fine\-tuning, LLM routing, Prompt engineering, RAG, Embeddings, Agentic workflows EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENTS\- 3–5 years of data science or ML engineering experience in an industry setting\- Demonstrated experience deploying models or AI systems to production (not just notebooks or prototypes)\- Hands\-on experience with at least two major LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral, Llama, etc.)\- Experience working within a client\-delivery or product team environment\- Familiarity with modern Python project structure and packaging (pyproject.toml, virtual environments) NICE TO HAVE\- Experience in telecom, networking, or infrastructure domains\- Familiarity with agentic frameworks (AutoGen, CrewAI, custom tool\-use patterns)\- Experience working with or alongside large tech clients\- Contributions to open\-source ML tooling WHAT WE'RE NOT LOOKING FOR This is not a research role. We are not seeking candidates whose primary experience is publishing papers, developing novel algorithms, or academic benchmarking. We need practitioners who have shipped and maintained AI systems in production.

title: Architects (x2) – Commercial Projects | Dublin URGENT
description: **Architects (x2\) – Commercial Projects \| Dublin URGENT** **Build the Future of Ireland's Commercial Landscape** IT Staff is excited to be recruiting two Architects to join a dynamic team delivering high\-profile commercial projects across Ireland. From office developments to retail, hospitality and mixed\-use schemes, this is a rare opportunity to work on landmark projects that will shape Ireland's built environment for generations to come **What You'll Be Doing** No two days will be the same. You'll be Leading commercial design, fit\-out, refurbishment, and new\-build projects across a diverse portfolio Collaborating with contractors, engineers, and specialist consultants throughout the project lifecycle Producing high\-quality AutoCAD drawings and contract documentation Driving projects from concept through to completion, both in the office and on\-site Working alongside a skilled design team to deliver innovative, high\-quality commercial spaces **What We're Looking For** MRIAI registered with 5–10 years' post\-graduation experience, including project and site management Strong design and technical drawing skills, with proficiency in AutoCAD A proven track record delivering commercial projects on time and within budget Experience across the full project lifecycle, from feasibility through to handover **Education \& Experience** Ideally, you'll bring A professional qualification in Architecture and full MRIAI registration A minimum of 5 years' and up to 10 years' experience working on commercial projects .Extra Points If You Have Experience on large\-scale or complex commercial developments Hands\-on involvement in fit\-out or new\-build commercial projects A proven ability to work in multi\-disciplinary teams and deliver results **Why Apply** Contract length: Initial 1\-year contract with extensions up to 3–4 years Flexibility: Hybrid working options available (Dublin base) Balance: 37\.4\-hour work week, Monday to Friday Impact: Your work will directly shape Ireland's commercial built environment on high\-profile, nationally significant projects **Interested** This is your chance to bring your architectural expertise to ambitious commercial projects at the forefront of Ireland's development landscape. If this sounds like the next step in your career, we'd love to hear from you. Please forward your most up\-to\-date CV for consideration .Seniority Level: Associate **Industry: Architecture and Planning** Employment Type: Contract Job Functions: Consulting \| Other Skills: Construction \| AutoCAD \| Commercial Architecture \| Architectural Design \| Project Management \| Contract Documentation \| Architects \| MRIAI

title: Architectural Graduate Training Programme
description: Location: Dublin Employer's Address Dublin Closing Date: 26 Jun 2026 Employer's Website https://www.riai.ie Employer's Email graduatetraining@riai.ie Programme Reference Number: OPW2026/01 The RIAI and the OPW are inviting applications for the Architectural Graduate Training Programme. The programme, designed for recent graduates with a recognised qualification in architecture, is an opportunity to obtain practical work experience for three years to prepare for the Professional Practice Examinations. The selection process will be shortlisting for competitive interview. The package for successful applicants consists of: * Salary €35,000\.00 per annum * Practical experience to prepare for the RIAI/ TU Dublin/ UCD Diploma in Professional Practice * 36 Month Placement in Dublin * Hours of work are: 37 hours per week 9:00am to 5:45pm on Monday to Thursday and 9:00am to 5:15pm on Friday with a break of one\-hour 15 minutes for lunch The Office of Public Works provides a wide range of architectural services to support the management of a significant part of the State’s property portfolio. Through a multi\-disciplinary service, the Office of Public Works delivers capital projects on behalf of the Government. This portfolio includes the conservation and protection of the State’s architectural heritage; construction of new environmentally sustainable buildings; specialist projects; the refurbishment, alteration, fit\-out and improvement to existing government office accommodation and provides advisory services to Government Departments and the public sector. In addition, the State Architect and Principal Architect Office of Public works supports the implementation of the National Policy on Architecture, approved by the Government in 2022\. The Office of Public Works is committed to providing enduring outcomes and generating value for many on behalf of its clients and users of its projects. Further to the requirements of the application form and guidance notes, can you please also provide the following: * Applicants should include a copy of a recent reference from an architect or college tutor. * Applicants to confirm and give details if they have previously worked for the OPW. * Applicants to confirm and give details if they have previously applied for the RIAI/OPW Architectural Graduate Training Programme. Closing Date for return of Applications is 12 Midday, Friday 26 June, 2026\. Application forms are available for download here. For further information, contact RIAI \+353 1 6761703 \| Email graduatetraining@riai.ie

title: Architectural Technologist
description: Location: Dublin Employer's Address Beech House, Beech Hill Office Camous, Beech Hill Road, Dublin 4 Closing Date: 09 Jul 2026 Employer's Website https://www.ksnpm.com/ Employer's Email hr@ksnpm.ie Job Description Established in 1998, KSNPM is a multi\-disciplinary and collaborative, forward thinking team of highly skilled and motivated professionals made up of Architects, Sustainability Consultants, Building Surveyors, and Project Managers. We are committed to design excellence, technical precision and creating a positive legacy through the built environment. We provide comprehensive, tailored advice to a wide range of clients and sectors. We are seeking an Architectural Technologist to join our rapidly expanding team. This position is ideal for a highly motivated professional with 5\-7 years’ post qualification experience. In this role you will be working with people from several disciplines with a strong focus on collaborating to provide excellent client service. You will be expected to take responsibility and attend site visits and design team/technical meetings as required. You will be working across an allocated portfolio of projects and will be ensuring that projects are adhering to all regulations and building standards. This will be across the whole life cycle of the project so you will be involved from inception to completion on all projects you work upon. The role offers an opportunity to work within a modern progressive and dynamic multi\-disciplinary consultancy as we continue to develop and expand the range of services that we deliver for our valued clients. KSNPM is a people focused business. We very much value the considerable effort that our team put in to ensure the successful delivery of our projects and the services that we offer. This is recognised and rewarded accordingly. Job Requirements Architectural Technologists are senior contributors within the design team, responsible for the technical delivery of planning, tender and construction documentation. * Experienced Architectural Technologist with a minimum of 5\-7 years post\-graduate experience * Strong leadership, communication and team building skills * Advanced Proficiency in Revit and CAD is essential. * Possess a working knowledge of Irish Planning and Building Control Legislation (BCMS) * Deep understanding on Building Regulations, fire safety, life safety and technical documentation * Proficiency in Microsoft Office and Adobe Creative Suite * Ability to operate effectively across all project stages and within multi\-disciplinary teams. * Mentor and coach junior staff to foster their growth and elevate team performance * An ambitious and can\-do attitude with a degree of flexibility to accommodate all responsibilities. * Excellent organisational skills with an ability to prioritise and work on multiple and varied projects * Own car and a full, clean Irish driving licence Remuneration: * Generous salary based on experience * Performance related Bonus * 21 Days Annual Leave plus min 10 Statutory Days * Pension Scheme * CPD and Training To apply, please send your CV and Portfolio by email to hr@ksnpm.ie Please note that our head office is in Clonskeagh, Dublin 4\. This position will be based in our Clonskeagh Office, Dublin.

title: Assistant Financial Controller
description: **Job Ref:** DAL5016**Branch:** Clayton Hotel Dublin Airport Central**Location:** Clayton Hotel Dublin Airport Central**Salary/Benefits:** Competitive Salary**Contract type:** Permanent**Hours:** Full Time**Shift pattern:** Flexible**Hours per week:** 39**Posted date:** 28/05/2026**Closing date:** 13/07/2026 **Assistant Financial Controller** Join Dalata Hotel Group, Ireland’s largest hotel group, as an Assistant Financial Controller. In this pivotal role, you will support the Financial Controller in delivering robust financial controls, accurate reporting, and commercial insights that drive operational excellence. This is an exciting opportunity to grow your career within a dynamic and expanding organisation. As the Assistant Financial Controller, you will help ensure the efficient operation of the hotel’s finance function. Reporting to the Financial Controller, General Manager, and other departments to achieve business objectives. **Benefits** * Free employee meals on duty * 50% Off FB in all properties * €/£60 employee rate on rooms for all Dalata owned hotels * 30% off the Friends Family rate * Extra AL day for every year worked (5 days to a maximum of 5 days) * Employee Assistance Programme * Cycle to Work Scheme/Transport Schemes * Pension access (Ask your HR Manager for local pension information) * Development Opportunities through our Dalata Academy to support your career journey **Your Responsibilities:** **Commercial Focus** * Manage day\-to\-day finance operations and ensure compliance with Dalata policies. * Prepare accurate monthly financial reports and perform balance sheet reconciliations. * Oversee income audit and weekly bank reconciliations. * Assist with internal audits and ensure corrective actions are implemented promptly. * Prepare VAT returns and support budget and forecast processes. * Drive cost control initiatives and provide commercial insights to hotel management. * Mentor and train finance team members and graduates. **People Focus** * Support and become a key advisor to both the Financial Controller and the General Manager. * Assist in writing and implementing training plans and courses for non\-financial managers within the hotel. * In conjunction with the Financial Controller, help to gain a better understanding of financial issues. * Ensure they are approachable and build relationships with the other Heads of Department and staff within the hotel. **Controls and Compliance** * Ensuring all financial controls and procedures across the hotel are robust, sustainable, maintained and updated. * Internal Audit Reports and agree corrective actions. * Champion the use of technology and more effective and efficient ways of doing business. **Month End** * Prepare the month\-end lead schedule. * Reconcile balance sheet control purpose. * Manage the collection of debts on a timely basis. **Requirements**: * Qualified or part\-qualified accountant (ACA, ACCA, CIMA), although not essential * Strong knowledge of accounting to drive improvements. **About Us** Dalata Hotel Group is an industry\-leading group of 56 hotels across Clayton, Maldron and other brands. At Dalata, you’ll find opportunities to develop your talent, be recognised for your commitment, and pursue a fulfilling career. We offer industry\-leading training programmes and a supportive, fair working environment. Dalata Hotel Group is an Equal Opportunities Employer. At The Clayton Hotel Dublin Airport Central , we are driven by our brand promise: Yes I Can! – delivering exceptional service with every guest interaction. As part of the globally trusted Clayton Hotels brand, we embody a culture of professionalism, hospitality, and integrity. Our team takes pride in creating memorable moments through warm, genuine, and thoughtful service. If you're passionate about hospitality and want to grow with a brand that values people, purpose, and performance, Clayton Hotel Dublin Airport Central is the right place for you.

title: Assistant Production Supervisor
description: **Production Supervisor Assistant** **Location:** Citywest, Dublin **Hours:** 40 hours per week **Shift:** Monday to Friday, starting at 4:00 AM **Industry:** Industrial Bakery Manufacturing **About Us** We are a fast\-paced industrial bakery based in Citywest, dedicated to producing high\-quality bakery products while maintaining the highest standards of food safety, quality, and operational efficiency. We are seeking a motivated and reliable Production Supervisor Assistant to support the daily operation of our production facility. **Role Overview** The Production Supervisor Assistant will work closely with the Production Supervisor to ensure production targets are achieved safely, efficiently, and to the highest quality standards. The successful candidate will assist with machine setup, production planning, quality checks, and general supervision of production activities throughout the shift. **Key Responsibilities** * Assist with the daily startup and setup of production lines and bakery equipment. * Support production planning and organisation to ensure efficient workflow throughout the shift. * Conduct hourly production and quality checks, ensuring products meet company specifications. * Monitor machine performance and report any issues to the Production Supervisor or Maintenance team. * Ensure production records, quality documentation, and check sheets are completed accurately. * Carry out routine operational and hygiene inspections across the production area. * Support troubleshooting and problem\-solving during production. * Ensure compliance with food safety, health \& safety, and quality standards. * Assist with staff coordination and communication on the production floor. * Help minimise waste and maximise production efficiency. * Promote a positive, quality\-focused working environment. **Requirements** * Previous experience in a manufacturing, food production, or bakery environment. * Strong attention to detail and commitment to product quality. * Good organisational and communication skills. * Ability to work effectively in a fast\-paced production environment. * Basic understanding of production machinery and manufacturing processes. * Ability to work independently and as part of a team. * Flexibility and reliability, with availability to start work at 4:00 AM. **Desirable** * Experience in a supervisory or team leader role. * Knowledge of HACCP, food safety **What We Offer** * Competitive salary. * Full\-time permanent position. * Opportunities for training and career progression. * Supportive team environment. * Opportunity to work with a growing and quality\-focused bakery business. Pay: From €15\.50 per hour Benefits: * On\-site parking Work Location: In person

title: Associate Compliance Specialist
description: **The Opportunity:** The Portfolio Management Compliance (PMC") Associate (Associate") is responsible for helping to support Portfolio Management Compliance team tasks, requirements, and protocols within the Fisher Group of companies. The PMC Associate works cross\-functionally with the members of the Fisher European Entities Legal and Compliance Department (LCD") to help them satisfy their portfolio management regulatory requirements. The Associate is a member of the Portfolio Management Compliance team, which acts as primary liaison between the Portfolio Management Group (PMG) and LCD with regard to all Fisher European Entities Portfolio Management policies, procedures, and regulatory requirements. **The Day\-to\-Day:** * Oversees assigned operational tasks to completion * Identifies, creates and develops documentation for group * Supports the growth of the business through various projects * Follows group and firm policies and procedures * Identifies potential process improvements and recommends solutions * Identifies elevations and routes appropriately * Responsible for properly handling sensitive and/or confidential documents Additional responsibilities may include: * Follows documented policies and procedures of the Portfolio Management Compliance team to competently, effectively, and efficiently meet regulatory reporting requirements * Monitor established controls to ensure all required trade reporting obligations to local regulators of the Fisher European Entities are being met in a timely and accurate manner according to MiFIR and EMIR guidelines, elevating to a senior member of the team if issues arise * Responsible for the timely completion of trade exception reports * Executes on internal trade process adherence reporting, elevating flags as needed * Responsible for all Fisher European Entities Trading Practices Committees documentation and meeting minutes **Your Qualifications:** * University Degree or equivalent combination of education and experience required * 1\+ years of experience in investment management regulatory compliance preferred * Working knowledge of European regulatory framework, particularly EMIR and MiFID II * Proven experience of working both independently and in a team\-oriented, collaborative environment * Ability to conform to shifting priorities, demands and timelines through analytical and problem\-solving capabilities * Excellent oral and written communication skills * Working knowledge of MS Office and Salesforce is preferable * Able to interface with different departments within the company **Why Fisher Investments Ireland:** The global Fisher organisation distinguishes itself by putting clients first, providing unmatched service, and taking a personalised approach to investing. You can feel confident knowing that Fisher Investments and Fisher Investments Ireland align with our clients' priorities by using a simple and transparent fee structure and recognised European custodians. It's the people that make the Fisher purpose possible, and to help our employees meet their long\-term goals, we offer an array of benefits, including: * 100% paid premiums for our top\-tier supplemental healthcare and dental plans for employees and their qualified dependents * 28 days annual leave, with the ability to purchase up to 3 additional days per year, plus up to 11 paid holidays * Enhanced maternity pay package with 26 weeks' top up to full base pay for eligible employees * $10,000\* fertility, hormonal health and family\-forming benefit * A retirement pension plan, featuring a 9% company pension contribution of your base pay and an additional company match of up to 5% of base pay * Monthly gym subsidy of up to ₠50 per month * Employee Assistance Program and other emotional wellbeing services * A collaborative working environment that practices ongoing training, educational support and employee appreciation events * This is an in\-office role. Based on your role, tenure, and performance eligibility you may have the opportunity to participate in our hybrid work from home program. This program is subject to change. \*Employees residing outside of the US will be eligible for the $10,000 equivalent in their local currency. FISHER INVESTMENTS IRELAND IS AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER

title: Associate Partner
description: **Location:** Limerick (hybrid/flexible working) **Type:** Senior leadership opportunity \| Long\-term progression potential Nicholas de Jong Associates is an established and respected land planning and urban design consultancy with a strong reputation for delivering high\-quality advice across regeneration, town centres, placemaking and development projects. We operate throughout Ireland, supporting predominantly public sector clients on projects ranging from strategic planning and master planning to regeneration strategies, urban design and public realm initiatives. As the practice enters its next phase of growth and development, we are seeking an **Associate Partner** to join the business in a senior strategic role and help shape its future direction. This is an opportunity for an experienced professional who combines technical credibility with commercial awareness and an ambition to contribute beyond project delivery. The successful candidate will work closely with the Principal, taking an active role in client relationships, business development, project leadership and the continued growth of the practice. The role offers the opportunity for increasing strategic responsibility and involvement in the long\-term development and continuity of the business. **The Role** * Lead and manage a portfolio of land planning, public realm and development projects * Build and strengthen long\-standing client relationships * Identify and develop new business opportunities * Contribute to practice management and strategic decision\-making * Support and coordinate team members and sub\-consultants * Represent the practice externally across professional and business networks **About You** You may already operate at Associate, Director or Partner level and will bring: * Significant experience within planning, urban design, regeneration or a related consultancy environment * Strong commercial and client\-facing capability * Proven track record of delivering and winning work * Excellent communication and leadership skills * Entrepreneurial mindset with an interest in helping shape a growing practice * Chartered status or equivalent professional standing **What We Offer** * Opportunity to join an established consultancy with an excellent reputation * Senior leadership role with genuine influence * Flexible working arrangements * Competitive package aligned with experience * Long\-term career progression and the opportunity to play a meaningful role in the future evolution of the business If you are interested in combining professional leadership with the opportunity to help shape the future of an established consultancy, we would welcome a confidential conversation. To express interest, please contact: Nicholas de Jong directly or email office@ndj.ie

title: Automation Engineer – OT Cyber Security & System Recovery
description: **Summary:** Our client, a leading pharmaceutical company based in North Wicklow, is seeking an Automation Engineer to support critical OT Cyber Security, System Recovery, and Automation initiatives across the site. The successful candidate will play a key role in strengthening the site's cyber security posture, improving disaster recovery readiness, implementing robust backup and restore processes, and supporting the deployment of Octoplant. In addition, this position will provide day\-to\-day automation support to ensure the continued reliability, availability, and compliance of automation systems within a GMP\-regulated manufacturing environment. **Responsibilities:** * Support the execution of the site's OT Cyber Security Remediation Programme, including vulnerability remediation and patch management activities. * Implement and maintain cyber security controls and best practices for OT systems, ensuring compliance with site and global standards. * Support governance activities including user access management, system hardening, and secure configuration management. * Collaborate with global cyber security teams to support remediation tracking, reporting, and implementation activities. * Develop and implement structured backup, restore, and disaster recovery strategies for critical automation systems. * Execute and document disaster recovery testing activities to ensure system recoverability and business continuity. * Maintain validation\-compliant processes for backup, restore, and disaster recovery activities. * Drive continuous improvement initiatives to enhance system recovery readiness and audit preparedness. * Support the deployment, configuration, and integration of Octoplant for centralised backups, version control, asset visibility, and lifecycle management. * Develop and maintain procedures for system versioning, backup management, and change tracking. * Support user training and onboarding activities associated with Octoplant implementation. * Define and implement controlled patch management processes across OT infrastructure. * Coordinate patching activities across PLC, SCADA, HMI, and supporting automation systems. * Develop and maintain SOPs and work instructions covering patching, backup and restore activities, and disaster recovery testing. * Ensure all activities are executed in accordance with GMP, change control, validation, and quality requirements. * Provide day\-to\-day support for site automation systems to ensure maximum uptime, reliability, and performance. * Support troubleshooting, incident investigation, root cause analysis, and corrective actions for automation\-related issues. * Manage automation system user access requests, backup requests, and restoration activities. * Work closely with equipment vendors and technical support teams to resolve system issues and implement improvements. * Support automation projects, system upgrades, and continuous improvement initiatives across the site. * Contribute to project documentation, FAT, SAT, IQ, OQ, and validation activities as required. * Ensure all activities comply with site quality systems, regulatory requirements, and engineering standards. **Qualifications \& Experience:** * Degree in Automation Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, or a related discipline. * A minimum of 5 years’ experience in an Automation Engineering role within a GMP\-regulated manufacturing environment. * Strong experience supporting OT systems, including PLCs, SCADA, HMIs, industrial networks, and associated infrastructure. * Demonstrated experience in OT Cyber Security, backup and recovery solutions, system patching, and lifecycle management. * Experience supporting automation projects, troubleshooting complex systems, and working within change control and validation frameworks. * Strong knowledge of GAMP5, ISA/IEC standards, 21 CFR Part 11, Annex 11, and GxP requirements. * Experience within the pharmaceutical, biotechnology, or other regulated manufacturing industries. * Experience with backup and version control platforms such as Octoplant, or similar tools, would be advantageous. * Industrial cyber security certifications or experience implementing OT Cyber Security and disaster recovery programmes would be advantageous.

title: Bioprocess Associate
description: ### **Job Information** Date Opened 06/11/2026 Job Type Full time Industry Pharma/Biotech/Clinical Research Work Experience 1\-3 years City Dublin State/Province Dublin Country Ireland ### **Job Description** **About PSC Biotech** **Who are we?** PSC Biotech is a leading Biotech Consultancy firm founded in 1996, headquartered in Pomona, California, USA, with Global operations in Ireland, India, Singapore, Australia and the US, serving 350 clients in more than 23 countries worldwide. We provide cloud\-based software solutions for Quality Management and Regulatory Inspections, pharmaceuticals contract manufacturing professionals, and metrology services to our clients. **‘Take your Career to a new Level’** PSC Biotech disrupts the conventional consultancy model by aligning our EVP as one of the unique selling point which includes the opportunity to work with the most talented cohort of like\-minded professionals operating in the Pharma/ Biotech Industry. We offer a permanent contract of employment giving exposure of working in Top Pharmaceutical client sites in a diverse\-cultural work setting. **Employee Value Proposition** Employees are the “heartbeat” of PSC Biotech, we provide unparalleled empowering career development though Learning \& Development in\-house training mentorship through constant guidance to facilitate career progression. We believe in creating high performing teams that can exceed our client’s expectations with regards to quality of all scalable and business unit deliverables, staying under budget and ensuring timelines for our deliverables are being met. **Overview:** * The BTA is responsible for all procedures and processes associated with the manufacture of Drug Substance at manufacturing facility, at the highest standards of Safety, Quality and Compliance. * The BTA will be responsible for undertaking manufacturing operations involved in the production of biologic drug substance on their designated shift. * Key responsibilities include the execution of an automated recipe using paperless technology to progress the drug substance through inoculation and cell culturing activities, leading to filtration and purification of the product in a state\-of\-the\-art single use technology\-based facility. The incumbent will be involved in setting up the single use * equipment between runs, carrying out the production process according to world class GMP and EHS principles. The BTA will also be involved in driving to optimise and improve the processing activities through use of standard work and lean processing techniques while supporting operations team to consistently deliver on specific Key Performance Indicators (KPIs). At all times, this role is focused on delivery to the patient through living the culture including a commitment to safety, focusing on right first time in everything we do and a concentrating on the engagement and development of self and peers. * The Manufacturing Biotech Associate will work in a manufacturing self\-directed shift team to deliver batch operations successfully, identify and resolve issues in order to allow the site to deliver for our patients in a culture that supports Quality, EHS, Learning and Continuous improvement. **Shift Pattern: 24/7** ### **Requirements** **Responsibilities:** * The production of biologic drug substance on their designated shift team and will be responsible for the preparation, operation and monitoring of equipment, use of business systems and carrying out tasks including but not limited to the cleaning, validation, in\-process testing. * The progression of an automated recipe using paperless technology in order to generate the drug substance through inoculation and cell culturing activities directing to filtration and purification of the product. * Setting up the single use equipment between runs, carrying out the production process according to world class GMP and EHS standards and driving to optimise and improve the processing activities through use of standard work and lean processing techniques. * Focusing on delivery to the patient through living the culture including a commitment to safety, focusing on right first time in everything we do and concentrating on the engagement and development of self and peers. **Skills Required:** * A science qualification in a technical, engineering or science\-based discipline or equivalent. * 0 to 3 years’ experience in the biotechnology and/or pharmaceutical industry or equivalent. * Experience in a highly regulated pharmaceutical manufacturing environment * Good understanding in the production of biologic drug substance and experience an advantage. * Ability to work as part of a shift team and on own initiative in a constructive manner. * Ability to think logically and be proactive under pressure. * Flexible and self\-motivated

title: Blockchain Platform Engineer
description: We are looking for Blockchain Platform Engineer with Blockchain/Web3 and infrastructure experience to join our client’s growing team in Dublin. You’ll build, deploy, and operate production blockchain systems. The role combines smart contract development with node and infrastructure operations, focusing on reliability, security, and scalable delivery of decentralized applications. **The Job** * Design, develop, and maintain smart contracts using Solidity or similar languages. * Build backend components that interact with blockchain networks, including transaction handling, signing flows, nonce and gas management, and Web3 SDK integrations. * Develop and maintain deployment pipelines for smart contracts, including versioning, migrations, and upgrade strategies. * Implement secure contract patterns and follow best practices for decentralized application development. * Deploy and operate blockchain nodes and supporting infrastructure, including RPC endpoints and validator nodes where applicable. * Manage node upgrades, network configuration changes, and production incidents. * Implement monitoring and operational tooling for node health, RPC performance, logging, and alerting. * Address blockchain reliability concerns such as chain reorganizations, finality, connectivity, and failover strategies. **Profile** * Strong understanding of blockchain fundamentals (transactions, blocks, wallets/keys, gas/fees, RPC, consensus concepts). * Hands\-on experience developing smart contracts with Solidity and common development frameworks. * Experience working with EVM\-compatible or similar blockchain platforms. * Solid background in software engineering and infrastructure operations. * Experience supporting production blockchain systems or Web3 applications. Based in Dublin City Centre. Permanent Role. Permanent Role. They offer competitive salaries. Benefits: Annual Bonus Scheme. Contributory Pension. Private Medical Insurance. Life Assurance \& Long\-Term Disability. Employee Assistance Programme. 22 days annual leave \+ 10 public holidays. Continuous Learning \& Development. Access to extensive training \& certification resources. Lunch \& Learn sessions. Company discounts, on\-site parking, and bike\-to\-work scheme The candidate must be eligible to work in Ireland/EU. Please do not hesitate to Contact David Coyle at 01 6351748 or email david@methodius.com

title: Business & Test Analyst
description: **At Ayvens, progress starts with you.** Our ambitions to shape the future of sustainable mobility are powered by our talent. Join us, and get better with every move. Reporting to the Head of Transformation \& Strategic Change and working with multi\-functional business \& IT teams this role combines business analysis with leadership of testing activities across projects. You will work closely with business stakeholders, data teams, and technical teams to define requirements, improve processes, and ensure high quality delivery of solutions. Acting as a key connector across functions and locations, you will ensure requirements are clearly defined, solutions are validated, and testing efforts are coordinated effectively across initiatives. **What will you be doing?** **Business Analysis \& Requirements** * Engage stakeholders to understand business processes and gather requirements * Document clear business and functional requirements (user stories, acceptance criteria) * Facilitate workshops and align business needs with proposed solutions * Ensure traceability between requirements, solutions, and outcomes **Process \& Data Analysis** * Analyse \& document business processes, workflows, and data flows * Identify gaps, risks, and opportunities for improvement * Support data mapping and collaborate with technical teams on solution design **Testing Coordination \& Quality Assurance** * Lead and coordinate testing activities across projects * Define test approach, plans, and scenarios aligned with requirements * Ensure coverage across functional, integration, system, and UAT phases * Support and guide teams during test execution, resolving blockers * Manage defects, track progress, and ensure timely resolution * Provide clear testing status updates, risks, and recommendations (e.g. Go/No\-Go) **Stakeholder Engagement** * Build strong relationships across business and technical teams globally * Communicate clearly with both technical and non‑technical audiences * Ensure alignment on priorities, progress, and outcomes **Continuous Improvement** * Promote best practices in analysis and testing * Contribute to process improvements and lessons learned **What would our ideal candidate have?** * 4\-8 years of combined business analysis \& testing experience across full end\-to\-end project life\-cycle, preferably within insurance or financial services. * Solid experience in business analysis (requirements gathering, process mapping \& functional documentation) * Solid experience leading or coordinating testing activities across projects * Good understanding of Agile methodologies (user stories, acceptance criteria) \& * Knowledge of testing lifecycle, defect management, and UAT coordination * Understanding of data analysis and system integration concepts * Excellent communication, collaboration and stakeholder management skills * Proactive, structured, logical, detail\-oriented approach and delivery‑focused mindset * Strong organisational and problem‑solving abilities * Fluent English (C1 or above) * Third level qualification is required; business analysis or testing certification is advantageous. **Nice to have** * Experience with Jira, Confluence, Azure DevOps, or similar tools * Exposure to data‑driven or regulated environments (Finance, Insurance, etc.) * Understanding of cloud or analytical platforms (e.g. Azure) * Experience of waterfall methodologies **Why Ayvens?** With over 3\.4 million vehicles managed across more than 42 countries, we provide full\-service leasing, flexible subscription services, fleet management services and multi\-mobility solutions to customers of all sizes, including large corporates, SMEs, professionals, and private individuals. By leveraging our unique position to lead the way to net zero and further shape the digital transformation of the industry, we are well\-positioned to meet the evolving mobility needs of our clients and provide them with the solutions they need to thrive. At Ayvens, we believe that our success is driven by our commitment to customer satisfaction. Our team is dedicated to delivering innovative solutions and technology\-enabled services that help our customers focus on their everyday business. We’re committed to sustainable mobility and have made it a core part of our strategy. In everything we do, we’re guided by the principles of authenticity, curiosity, commitment and collaboration. We aim to foster an organisation that's diverse in people and ideas, where everyone can thrive and be themselves, no matter who they are. Join us on this exciting journey as we continue to enable the transformation towards large scale adoption of sustainable mobility and provide our customers with the solutions they need to succeed. Follow our page for the latest updates, news, and insights.

title: Business Application Support Compliance Contract Dublin
description: Business Application Support Compliance Contract Dublin 6\-12 Months. This role provides end\-to\-end application management and production support for a suite of Compliance\-facing applications, with a primary focus on Communication Surveillance, Trade Surveillance, Employee Conflicts of Interest, and related regulatory platforms, and their associated processes. You will conduct batch monitoring performing daily checks and oversight of key scheduled activities. You will get involved in change management and working with the business to understand issues and collaborating with product teams, development teams, and vendors to deliver effective solutions. Project support \- serving as an SME for Compliance applications and ensuring project deliverables are released into production in a controlled and compliant manner. Release management; provide support for system enhancements across PROD / REL / QA / UAT environments. Infrastructure coordination; working closely with infrastructure teams to maintain stable and reliable business platforms. title: Business Developer Project Intern (TikTok Shop) - 2026 Start (BS/MS)

title: Business Development Representative
description: Fin is the AI Customer Agent company on a mission to help businesses provide perfect customer experiences. Our AI Agent Fin is the highest\-performing AI Customer Agent on the market today, enabling businesses to deliver impeccable, always\-on customer support across the customer journey – from service, to sales, to ecommerce. Powered by our own AI models, Fin resolves complex customer issues end\-to\-end across every channel, with minimal set\-up and integration. Fin can also be combined with our natively integrated Intercom help desk for one single system that is designed to meet the needs of modern day support teams. Founded in 2011, Fin became one of the fastest growing companies and remains one of the largest private software companies in the world with nearly 30,000 global businesses using our products to transform their customer support. Driven by our core values, we push boundaries, build with speed and intensity, and relentlessly deliver incredible value to our customers. What's the opportunity? As a Business Development Representative, you will play a critical role in driving revenue growth for the Fin Sales organization through effective pipeline generation. Our role is to lay the foundation for Fin Sales by engaging with prospects and customers, helping them understand the value of Fin through creative \& engaging prospecting. We invest in our BDRs. We strongly believe in giving our team the opportunities to grow \& develop at Fin, to push themselves outside of their comfort zones to achieve goals they never thought possible. In joining the Business Development team at Fin, you join a community that believes in development and promotion from within. We're looking for people who are excited to work in a fast\-moving company, make an impact, and rapidly develop their career in sales. As a BDR team, we empower one another to achieve our goals, exceed expectations and continue to deliver real business impact. We're looking for a teammate to do the same! What will I be doing? * Creating and prioritizing strategic target account lists with Account Executives * Generating new business pipeline for Account Executives through cold calling, email, social etc. * Providing a great experience for Fin's prospective customers * Maintaining an up\-to\-date knowledge of our product and its value to customers * Demonstrating an ability to attract new customers * Working with other functions to improve business processes * Engaging in team development and mentoring What skills do I need? * Strong desire to build a career in Sales, you want to be an Account Executive or a closing based role in the future * 1\+ year of customer\-facing work experience (Sales and/or SaaS experience is a \+) * Operational Excellence: + You can use your time in an effective and efficient manner to complete revenue driving activities, you can quickly identify where to focus your efforts to drive more pipeline. * Competitive Landscape \& Industry Knowledge is crucial. + You'll bring an understanding of the SaaS landscape and our target verticals. You always want to stay on top of the latest news \& industry trends. * Prospecting skills (cold calling, email, social) + You know how to leverage modern sales engagement tools to deliver prospect engagement across multiple channels (phone, email, LinkedIn) * Communication + You can articulate your thoughts and express ideas effectively using verbal, written and non\-verbal communication skills (to inform, instruct, and persuade), to different audiences. + You listen effectively and love to partner and collaborate with your peers * Growth Mindset + You're self\-aware and understand both your strengths and weaknesses. You understand every day is an opportunity to be 1% better than the day before. You proactively seek feedback. * Results Oriented + You bring a never settle approach to quota, progression and team development. Results oriented SDRs are hungry to succeed and raise the bar. Bonus skills \& attributes * Bachelor's Degree preferred * Familiarity with these systems and tools: (SFDC, Outreach, Cognism, Zoominfo, LinkedIn Sales Navigator) **Benefits** We are a well treated bunch, with awesome benefits! If there's something important to you that's not on this list, talk to us! * Competitive salary and equity in a fast\-growing start\-up * We serve lunch every weekday, plus a variety of snack foods and a fully stocked kitchen * Regular compensation reviews \- we reward great work! * Pension scheme \& match up to 4% * Peace of mind with life assurance, as well as comprehensive health and dental insurance for you and your dependents * Flexible paid time off policy * Paid maternity leave, as well as 6 weeks paternity leave for fathers, to let you spend valuable time with your loved ones * If you're cycling, we've got you covered on the Cycle\-to\-Work Scheme. With secure bike storage too * MacBooks are our standard, but we also offer Windows for certain roles when needed. **Policies** Fin has a hybrid working policy. We believe that working in person helps us stay connected, collaborate easier and create a great culture while still providing flexibility to work from home. We expect employees to be in the office at least three days per week. We have a radically open and accepting culture at Fin. We avoid spending time on divisive subjects to foster a safe and cohesive work environment for everyone. As an organization, our policy is to not advocate on behalf of the company or our employees on any social or political topics out of our internal or external communications. We respect personal opinion and expression on these topics on personal social platforms on personal time, and do not challenge or confront anyone for their views on non\-work related topics. Our goal is to focus on doing incredible work to achieve our goals and unite the company through our core values. Fin values diversity and is committed to a policy of Equal Employment Opportunity. Fin will not discriminate against an applicant or employee on the basis of race, color, religion, creed, national origin, ancestry, sex, gender, age, physical or mental disability, veteran or military status, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, marital status, or any other legally recognized protected basis under federal, state, or local law.

title: Business Development Representative UKI
description: **Your work days are brighter here.** We’re obsessed with making hard work pay off, for our people, our customers, and the world around us.
