Our deep expertise enables our customers to navigate the rapidly evolving technology landscape. We foster strong partnerships with global technology leaders including Microsoft, AWS, Oracle, Red Hat, OutSystems, Snowflake, ensuring that our customers are provided with the highest quality solutions and services. We’re an award\-winning employer reflecting how our employees are at the very heart of what we do: * UK \& Ireland's premier AWS, Microsoft \& Oracle partner * 3300\+ strong, €350/£300m revenue business * 10\+ years as a Great Place to Work in Ireland \& UK * Best Workplace for Women in the UK \& Ireland by GPTW * Best Workplace for Wellbeing in the UK by GPTW We’re a core values driven company, we hire people who share our values, and we reward those who display and foster them, it’s deeply embedded within our DNA. Invest in us and we’ll invest in you! **Job Description** We are seeking experienced Data Engineers to join our Data \& Analytics development team to support a large\-scale data modernisation programme. This initiative focuses on migrating the existing analytics platform from Oracle to Microsoft Fabric. The selected candidates will work closely with an established Agile team and help advance delivery across several coordinated workstreams within an evolving delivery environment. **Key Responsibilities** * Support the migration of semantic models (lift\-and\-shift) into Microsoft Fabric * Design, develop, and improve data pipelines * Build and maintain metadata\-driven ingestion and orchestration frameworks * Perform integration testing to support data quality and system stability * Execute data mirroring activities from Oracle to Microsoft Fabric * Contribute to data modelling for analytics and reporting solutions * Support Microsoft Purview implementation, including: + Data scanning + Cataloguing + Governance enablement * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams across the data modernisation programme * Work within an Agile delivery environment, supporting consistent and high\-quality outcomes **Qualifications** **Required Skills \& Experience (Essential)** * Experience in Data Engineering, including hands\-on work with Microsoft Fabric * Experience designing and developing data pipelines * Experience with data modelling for analytics and reporting * Experience working with modern cloud\-based data platforms (data warehouse and lakehouse architectures) * Experience working within Agile delivery teams **Desirable Skills** * Experience with metadata\-driven ingestion frameworks and orchestration tools * Experience with Fabric mirroring capabilities and data replication technologies * Experience working on data platform modernisation initiatives * Experience with Microsoft Purview (scanning, cataloguing, governance) * Prior experience working with Oracle databases **Key Success Factors** * Ability to contribute effectively within a structured delivery environment * Ability to collaborate with team members across multiple workstreams * Ability to balance priorities across several areas of delivery * Focus on maintaining consistent delivery standards and scalability **Additional Information** At Version 1, we believe in providing our employees with a comprehensive benefits package that prioritises their wellbeing, professional growth, and financial stability. * Salary Range: €50,000\-€65,000 * Share in our success with our Quarterly Performance\-Related Profit Share Scheme, where employees collectively benefit from a share of our company's profits * Strong Career Progression \& mentorship coaching through our Strength in Balance \& Leadership schemes with a dedicated quarterly Pathways Career Development programme * Flexible/remote working, Version 1 is tremendously understanding of life events and people’s individual circumstances and offer flexibility to help achieve a healthy work life balance * Financial Wellbeing initiatives including; Version 1 Pension with a 5% employee\-matched contribution, Private Healthcare Cover, Life Assurance, Financial advice and an Employee Discount scheme * Employee Wellbeing schemes including Gym Discounts, Bike to Work, Fitness classes, Mindfulness Workshops, Employee Assistance Programme and much more. * 24 days holiday allowance plus Public Holidays, enhanced maternity/paternity leave, marriage/civil partnership leave and special leave policies * Educational assistance, incentivised certifications, and accreditations, including AWS, Microsoft, Oracle, and Red Hat * Reward schemes including Version 1’s Annual Excellence Awards \& quarterly Star Awards. * Environment, Social and Community First initiatives allow you to get involved in local fundraising and development opportunities as part of fostering our diversity, inclusion and belonging schemes. And many more exciting benefits… drop us a note to find out more. Version 1 is an equal opportunities employer We are committed to building a diverse, inclusive and respectful workplace where everyone feels valued and able to thrive. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, identities and lived experiences, and we value the different perspectives people bring including those shaped by disability and neurodiversity. We want every candidate to have a positive and accessible recruitment experience. If you need reasonable adjustments at any stage of the process, please contact the recruiter Ekta Bahl at ekta.bahl@version1\.com. title: Data Quality Analyst
description: **About us:** Amach is an industry\-leading technology driven company with headquarters located in Dublin and remote teams in UK and Europe. Our blended teams of local and nearshore talent are optimised to deliver high quality and collaborative solutions. Founded in 2013, Amach was created to solve a specific problem in aviation: too much complexity, too little usable intelligence. We help airlines modernise their operating model using cloud, data and Al\-delivered by teams with deep aviation domain expertise. Our goal is to maximize airlines' operational efficiency by optimizing resource use, reduce costs and increase customer experience and satisfaction. We are seeking a **Data Quality Analyst** to be embedded with our customer, where you will drive improvements in data quality, trust, and accountability across their organisation. Working closely with the customer's teams, you will identify critical data issues, define and monitor quality rules, investigate root causes, and partner with data owners and stewards to deliver sustainable improvements. This is a hands\-on role that combines data quality management, governance, and stakeholder engagement to support the customer's strategic data objectives. ***Please note** : this is a fully remote role.* **Key responsibilities \& duties include:** * Gather and document business requirements for data quality monitoring and governance initiatives working with the customer's stakeholders * Support the triage, prioritisation, investigation, and remediation of data quality issues and incidents across the customer's data landscape * Assist with the maintenance, adoption, and continuous improvement of the customer's enterprise data catalogue * Build strong working relationships with the customer's business stakeholders, data owners, and data stewards to support data quality improvement initiatives * Manage stakeholder communications throughout the lifecycle of data quality projects and initiatives with the customer's teams * Analyse the customer's existing databases, systems, and data sources to identify opportunities for improving data quality * Produce clear and comprehensive documentation, including business requirements, data quality rules, metrics, and reports * Collaborate closely with the customer's Agile delivery teams, including Product Owners, Data Engineers, and Product Managers * Contribute to the continuous improvement of the customer's data governance processes, standards, and operating procedures * Support governance activities relating to audit, risk, compliance, and regulatory requirements * Measure and report on data quality KPIs and business outcomes for the customer's leadership * Support the implementation and adoption of data governance policies and standards across the customer's organisation **Required Experience:** * Minimum 3\+ years' experience in a Data Analyst, Data Quality Analyst, Data Governance, or related role * Strong SQL and data analysis capabilities * Experience working with data quality management and data governance programmes * Experience identifying, investigating, and resolving data quality issues * Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills with the ability to influence and collaborate across multiple groups * Experience creating business and technical documentation * Ability to facilitate workshops and work effectively with both business and technical teams * Strong organisational skills with excellent attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy * Proven ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver high quality work to deadlines * Self motivated, proactive, and comfortable working in a fast paced environment * Strong analytical and problem solving skills * Excellent verbal and written communication skills * Positive, collaborative, and solution focused approach * Ability to work independently while contributing effectively as part of a team * Relevant third level qualification in Data Analytics, Computer Science, Information Systems, Mathematics, Engineering, or a related discipline **Nice to have:** * Experience with Data Observability and Data Quality tools such as Monte Carlo, Soda, Informatica, or similar platforms * Experience working with Data Catalogue platforms such as Atlan, Collibra, Microsoft Purview, or Databricks Unity Catalog * Experience within finance, customer, operational, or enterprise data environments * Experience working in Agile delivery environments * Knowledge of AI governance frameworks and best practices * Experience using Jira and Confluence * Experience with Power BI, Snowflake, Databricks, and modern cloud data platform **What’s in it for you:** * An opportunity to join a fast\-growing company * Options for career advancement * Learning and development opportunities * Flexible working environment * Competitive salaries based on experience **Equal Opportunity Employer:** Amach is an equal opportunity employer and makes employment decisions on the basis of merit. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. This job description is intended to convey essential responsibilities and qualifications for this role, but it is not an exhaustive list of tasks that an employee may be required to perform. If you are passionate about driving customer success, advising on strategic solutions, and contributing to product innovation, we would love to hear from you! **Not for you?** Check out all of our open positions in our **careers page** and follow us on **LinkedIn** for future opportunities. P.S. Share this with friends and co\-workers! Don't be afraid they'll steal it from you, if you're amazing and smart we'll find a role for you. We are growing fast and we are always looking for talented people. At Amach, we strive to be an inclusive community of open\-minded individuals with different backgrounds and we are committed to fostering, cultivating and preserving a culture of diversity, equity and inclusion. We strongly believe that a diversity of experience and background is essential to create a fulfilling environment and better solutions for our people and our customers. All Amach employees and contractors are expected to honour this policy and act to ensure that every individual is respected in the workplace. **Your personal data** Amach will process your personal information in accordance with the EU's General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). We will comply with data protection law and principles, which means that your data will be: * Used lawfully, fairly and in a transparent way * Collected only for valid purposes and not used in any way that is incompatible with those purposes * Relevant to the purposes we have told you about and limited only to those purposes * Accurate and kept up to date * Kept only as long as necessary for the purposes we have told you about * Kept securely If you would like to contact us about your data, please use the following address: info@amach.com

title: Database Software Engineer
description: JOB DESCRIPTION At eBay, we're more than a global ecommerce leader — we’re changing the way the world shops and sells. Our platform empowers millions of buyers and sellers in more than 190 markets around the world. We’re committed to pushing boundaries and leaving our mark as we reinvent the future of ecommerce for enthusiasts. Our customers are our compass, authenticity thrives, bold ideas are welcome, and everyone can bring their unique selves to work — every day. We're in this together, sustaining the future of our customers, our company, and our planet. Join a team of passionate thinkers, innovators, and dreamers — and help us connect people and build communities to create economic opportunity for all. **PostgreSQL Database Engineer (with Software Engineering Expertise)** **Location:** Dublin, Ireland **Job Type:** Full\-time **About the team and the role** We are seeking a **Senior PostgreSQL Database Engineer** with strong software engineering expertise to design, optimize, and scale eBay’s database platforms while also contributing to backend development and automation tooling. This is a **hands\-on engineering role** that bridges data infrastructure and software systems — ideal for someone passionate about both PostgreSQL internals and building scalable, reliable services. You will collaborate closely with product, platform, and SRE teams to ensure our data infrastructure delivers performance, resilience, and compliance at global scale. **What you will accomplish** * Design and implement scalable, highly available PostgreSQL architectures, including replication, partitioning, and disaster recovery. * Improve query performance, indexing strategies, and database configurations following PostgreSQL guidelines. * Develop tools and services to automate provisioning, monitoring, and deployment of databases and related systems. * Implement and maintain strong database security standards, including role\-based access control and encryption. * Collaborate with software, DevOps, and analytics teams to integrate PostgreSQL solutions with applications and pipelines. * Mentor engineers and implement guidelines for schema development, tuning, and database operations. **What you will bring** * **7\+ years** of hands\-on experience managing PostgreSQL in large\-scale production environments. * Strong programming proficiency in **C/C\+\+, Python, Go, or Java**, with experience building automation or backend tools. * Expertise in **SQL and PL/pgSQL**, with a deep understanding of PostgreSQL internals and performance tuning. * Experience with **Linux/Unix**, **Kubernetes**, and containerized deployments (Docker). * Proven knowledge of replication, backup, and migration strategies, including cross\-platform migrations (e.g., Oracle PostgreSQL). * Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Software Engineering, Computer Science, or equivalent experience. **Location and work model** This role follows **eBay’s hybrid model**, requiring **three days per week in the Dublin office** (unless otherwise stated). **Additional Details** eBay is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, veteran status, and disability, or other legally protected status. If you have a need that requires accommodation, please contact us at talent@ebay.com. We will make every effort to respond to your request for accommodation as soon as possible. View our accessibility statement to learn more about eBay's commitment to ensuring digital accessibility for people with disabilities. We use cookies to enhance your experience and may use AI tools for administrative tasks in the hiring process. To learn how we handle your personal data and use AI responsibly, please visit our Talent Privacy Notice, Privacy Center, and AI Hiring Guidelines. title: Datacenter Technician

title: Dealer
description: A leading global asset management group is seeking an experienced Execution Dealer/Trader to join its Dublin\-based dealing team. **What You’ll Do** * Execute global equities trades against specific benchmarks in alignment with established investment strategies while strictly adhering to best execution policies. * Optimise order management and execution by utilising various trading strategies and algorithms to source appropriate liquidity across multiple markets. * Maintain rigorous compliance with regulatory requirements as well as internal risk and compliance rules throughout all trading activities. * Monitor best execution standards and conduct transaction cost analysis to ensure optimal outcomes for clients. * Liaise closely with Portfolio Managers and Portfolio Manager Assistants to provide key insights on market conditions, news flow, liquidity, and execution performance throughout the trading day. * Disseminate market intelligence to investment teams, adding value to the overall investment process through timely information sharing. * Coordinate with Middle Office colleagues to ensure timely settlement of all orders and effective cash management practices. * Manage broker relationships by conducting transaction cost analysis reviews and maintaining open communication channels for ongoing evaluation. * Execute FX Forward, FX Spot transactions, and futures trades as required by portfolio needs. * Work collaboratively with Compliance \& Risk Departments to report potential incidents or breaches promptly and resolve them Futures is necessary for handling complex portfolio needs. * Understanding principles of portfolio construction enables you to add value during trade execution processes. * Strong IT skills including proficiency with Bloomberg, Microsoft Office suite, OMS (Order Management System), EMS (Execution Management System), and other trading software providers are vital for efficient workflow. * Ability to adapt quickly under pressure ensures smooth functioning during volatile market conditions. * Exceptional attention to detail allows you to maintain accuracy across all aspects of trade execution and reporting. * Clear and concise communication skills facilitate effective interaction with Portfolio Managers, Middle Office staff, brokers, and compliance teams. * Strong organisational skills paired with problem\-solving abilities enable you to manage multiple tasks efficiently within tight deadlines. **What's Next** If you are ready to take your career as a Dealer to a globally respected asset management group that values collaboration and inclusivity, we encourage you to apply now. Apply today by clicking on the link provided. Robert Walters Operations Limited is an employment business and employment agency and welcomes applications from all candidates

title: Dental Hygienist
description: Dental Hygienist – Full\-Time or Part\-Time We are seeking a motivated and patient\-focused Dental Hygienist to join our team in of enthusiastic and supportive clinicians in a modern, fully equipped, high\-tech dental practice. title: DevOps Engineer (€60k)
description: Some companies use DevOps to ship apps faster. This team is using it to help reshape how future medicines are discovered. We’re working with a fast\-growing deep\-tech company operating at the intersection of AI, computational science, and pharmaceutical innovation. Now they’re looking for a DevOps Engineer who loves building systems from the ground up.
