The Equipment Process Engineer will also have responsibility for the reliability and life cycle of equipment. **Key Responsibilities:** * Support a safe working environment by complying with all pertinent environmental health/safety practice, rules and regulations * Ownership of equipment/systems in a GMP regulated manufacturing setting that support the Drug Product manufacturing processes * Monitoring of day\-to\-day performance, including trend analysis of all performance aspects of their system and equipment to identify and proactively manage performance trends. * Support system improvements, development of detailed specifications, engineering documents and standard operating procedures * Support technical root cause analysis, incident investigations and after action reviews when troubleshooting issues related to manufacturing equipment/systems * Solving complex problems, project management, equipment lifecycle management and operational excellence initiatives related to their systems / equipment * Develop and manage change control requests per established SOPs and processes * Support new product introductions or new technology introductions by performing engineering assessments, implementation of system changes and supporting engineering runs * Communicating with key stake holders, in a responsive manner to ensure they are kept informed on all engineering activities * Working in a collaborative manner within cross functional work centre team, and contributes to investigations, Non\-Conformance and CAPA closure. * Develop a structured maintenance schedule which will align with the manufacturing schedule to minimise downtime. This includes routine PM’s and long term planning for vendor support on site and the procurement of major spare part assemblies in a timely manner to support larger overhauls * Development and maintenance of spare parts lists for their system and appropriate levels of spares are maintained. **Win** **What we expect of you** The successful candidate for this role will bring the following education, skills and experience Basic Qualifications: * Bachelor's degree in Engineering (Mechanical, Manufacturing, Mechatronics, Electrical or Chemical). * Manufacturing experience in a regulated environment. * Experience in the use of statistical analysis tool \& continuous improvement methodologies. * Problem solving and applied engineering skills relating to mechanical, electro\-mechanical and associated automation systems. Preferred Qualifications: * Manufacturing experience in biopharmaceutical Formulation/Fill/Finish or Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient (API) facilities. * Technical writing, and communication/presentation skills. * Experience in change control, non\-conformance, corrective and preventative actions, and validation practices. * Experience in developing strategies for new product introduction and new technology deployment. * Support the translation of strategic opportunities and emerging technology solutions into tangible pragmatic executable plans. * Support the development of business area strategy and engineering/equipment strategy where appropriate. * Development and presentation of project plans to management. * Day to day vendor management. * Independent, self\-motivated, organized, able to multi\-task in project environments and skilled in communication and collaboration. * Team player, prepared to work in and embrace a team\-based culture that relies on collaboration for effective decision\-making. * Travel at various times may be required to support execution of projects. **Thrive** **What you can expect of us** In addition to a competitive salary structure, Amgen offers a generous Total Rewards Plan for staff and eligible dependents. Comprehensive employee benefits package, including retirement and savings plan with generous company contributions, group medical, life and disability insurance. Discretionary annual bonus program. Exceptional learning, development and career advancement opportunities, empowering employees to grow and build long\-term careers. At Amgen, we are committed to nurturing talent and providing meaningful pathways for professional development. *Amgen is an equal opportunities employer*

title: Event Marketing Specialist
description: **Company Description** Arista Networks pioneered data\-driven, cognitive cloud networking for large\-scale datacenter and campus environments. Arista's award\-winning platforms, ranging in Ethernet speeds from 10 to 400 gigabits per second, redefine scalability, agility and resilience. Arista has shipped more than 20 million cloud networking ports worldwide with CloudVision and EOS, an advanced network operating system. Committed to open standards, Arista is a founding member of the 25/50G consortium. Arista Networks products are available worldwide directly and through partners. **Job Description** We’re looking for an Event Marketing Specialist who is passionate about delivering exceptional event experiences. You’ll be a part of the EMEA Marketing team, leading the most impactful projects and programmes of work in support of Arista Network’s growing EMEA business. **Who You’ll Work With** The Arista Networks business has seen significant growth in the last few years, boasting several well\-known brands as customers. To help accelerate this growth, we’re now looking for an experienced event marketing specialist to help build, execute and scale out our events; and to drive clear prospect engagement and attributable revenue impact in the EMEA region. You’ll need to apply your experience of working with global teams to develop and execute local marketing strategies, measurement and GTM materials. You’ll need to be comfortable in solving challenges and establishing new ways of working. You won’t have to do this alone, as you’ll be supported by the wider EMEA marketing team and global marketing colleagues. Day\-to\-day, your primary focus will be to lead regional event marketing activities for EMEA \- supporting and running large\-scale owned and third\-party events, and locally supported field events. You will work cross\-functionally with other Arista teams to drive top of the funnel and ongoing engagement with customers at different stages of the sales cycle. Your efforts will directly impact our revenue growth by enabling our sales teams to sell more, better and faster. **What You’ll Do** Event Strategy \& Planning: * Planning, organising, and executing large\-scale marketing events to ensure clear promotion of our brand and services. * Lead other local owned events by creating, planning and executing new events such as roundtables, forums, workshops, and webinars . **Lead Management \& Reporting** * Track, analyse, and report on event performance against defined KPIs (e.g., leads generated, pipeline influenced, attendance, engagement). * Provide post\-event analysis and recommendations for continuous improvement. **Cross\-Functional Collaboration** * Act as a liaison between marketing and sales to ensure event objectives are met and sales teams are fully enabled for pre\- and post\-event activities. * Train and brief sales teams on event objectives, messaging, and lead qualification processes. **End\-to\-End Event Logistics \& Execution** * Manage all aspects of event logistics, including venue selection, all vendor negotiation and management, swag and other collateral orders, and registration platforms and event communications. **Qualifications** **About You:** * 3\-5\+ years’ experience in running and managing events. * Excellent project management, multitasking, and stakeholder alignment skills. * Proactive, commercially minded, and confident working remotely. * A confident communicator able to speak to colleagues and partners with conviction and clarity. * Excellent verbal and written communication skills and strive to have a customer\-first mindset. * Experience working in Tech Sector or B2B2B solutions targeting enterprise customers. * Self\-starter and impact\-driven: you enjoy taking ownership of projects with limited oversight. You thrive more on autonomy than structure. You’re not afraid to take responsibility and ownership of your work. You come ready equipped with perseverance and a sizable sense of humour. * Quick learner and problem solver with a strong analytical mindset and eager to learn and develop with a passion for using data to improve campaign performance. **Desirable Skillsets/experiences** * Experience in using HubSpot. * Experience in using Salesforce. * Experience working in an international organisation. * Previously worked in a start\-up environment. **Additional Information** All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.

title: Executive Assistant: International Government Affairs
description: *To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.* Job Category Corporate Functions Job Details **About Salesforce** Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all. Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce. Job Description: Executive Assistant (International Government Affairs) **Department Description** The International Government Affairs team is a diverse, high\-impact cohort of professionals managing the company’s strategic engagement with government officials, legislators, and regulators throughout EMEA, APAC, and ASEAN. Integrated within this group, the Global Public Policy team serves as a critical engine for shaping our corporate positions on evolving public policy issues. **Role Description** You will serve as the strategic partner to the Vice President of International Government Affairs (EMEA, APAC, ASEAN) and Global Public Policy. Your mission is to maximize leadership impact by navigating complex internal ecosystems, orchestrating global logistics, and ensuring a distributed 20\-person team remains synchronized across three continents. Beyond traditional calendar management, you will own the operational rhythm of a high\-velocity global department. **Core Responsibilities** * Internal Navigation \& Approvals * The "Fixer": Help manage all internal approval proceedings (contracts, headcount, budget requests, etc.). * Expense Management: Own the end\-to\-end expense process, ensuring accuracy across different currencies and compliance with company policy. * Communication Architecture * Information Flow: Ensure that key updates from the manager reach the team, and that urgent "bottom\-up" information reaches the manager. * Channels Management: Structure and manage how the team communicates and collaborates with the other teams. This includes optimizing Slack channels, managing email distribution lists, and ensuring docs are organized and accessible. * Cross\-Functional Bridge: Act as a connector between the Government Affairs team and other departments (Legal, PR, Finance) to ensure everyone is in the loop. Help develop a more cross\-functional approach across the team, aiming to leverage existing resources, build on existing expertise, and share accurate information to maximize impact and velocity. * Global Operations \& Logistics * Agenda: Manage a complex agenda by combining a high volume of internal and external stakeholder meetings, overseeing relationships with highly sensitive and multi cultural stakeholders (government officials, C\-Suite level), and helping to prioritize and manage agenda pressure. * Travel Coordinate: complex, multi\-leg international trips. You’ll handle the logistics (flights, cars, hotels). * Time Zone Logic: Manage a high\-volume calendar across EMEA, APAC, and ASEAN. You understand the "golden hours" where these regions overlap and protect those slots for critical syncs. * Team Coordination * Team Pulse: Organise team meetings and offsites that actually work for a distributed group. * Project Liaison: Work alongside our Cross\-Functional Project Manager. While they track the "policy wins," you track the "people and process," ensuring the Leader is prepared for every milestone. **Minimum Requirements** * Minimum of 5\+ years of applicable administrative experience, ideally from a global software organization. * Skillful execution of administrative activities, with high attention to detail, organization and process. * Experience with multi\-cultural teams. * Strong teamwork skills; and a confident ability to offer suggestions and improvements to process and work very collaboratively with others. * Strong relationship management skills, ability to build a personal network throughout the company. * Strong verbal and written communication. Ability to communicate clearly and effectively with senior leaders and external partners. * Proven track record of supporting executive leadership within dynamic, high\-growth environments, including high\-stakes engagement with senior public and private sector stakeholders. * Working proficiency of Google Suite (Gmail, Docs, Drive, GMeet). Experience using Quip, Slack, Salesforce and Tableau and are a plus. * Must be in one of the listed locations and open to being hybrid in office 3 days per week. **Additional Desired Skills/Traits** * Customer centric orientation. * Discreet \& Diplomatic: You handle sensitive information regarding government negotiations and internal ELT discussions with total confidentiality. * Agility and flexibility in response to changing priorities and needs. * Proven ability to manage projects, work under pressure and consistently make deadlines. * Demonstrate ability and willingness to continuously acquire new competencies and accept new challenges. * Possess an ability and curiosity and to comprehend the global DNA of the company and its organizations. * Ensure communications are clear, thorough, and specific, with attention to follow\-through. Unleash Your Potential When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world. Accommodations If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form. Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options. Posting Statement Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non\-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.

title: Expert Planning & Scheduling
description: Location: Leixlip, Ireland Category: Commercial Job ID: 22261 Permanent Exyte Northern Europe Ltd. (2002\) **Discover your exciting role** ------------------------------- Exyte are now hiring for a Planner on Exyte Data Center projects across Northern Europe. The successful candidate will be responsible for supporting project delivery through programme development, progress monitoring, risk management and reporting to senior management. This role will require strong 4D planner experience, including proficiency in “Synchro 4D” . **About our Data Centers (DTC) division** Exyte delivers comprehensive data center services from design to construction, ensuring efficiency and reliability. With our integrated project delivery approach, we have successfully executed numerous projects worldwide, including advanced intelligence data centers, hyperscale data centers, colocation and cloud\-computing data centers using traditional construction as well as modular construction techniques. **Explore your tasks and responsibilities** ------------------------------------------- * Lead 4D planning and visual simulation activities across one or multiple Data Centre projects. * Develop and manage integrated 4D models linking programme schedules (P6 / Asta) with BIM 3D models. * Establish standards for 4D sequencing, model integration, animation outputs, and digital governance aligned with project and corporate requirements. * Act as the primary 4D interface between Planning, BIM, Construction, and Project Controls teams. * Translate construction methodologies into logical, buildable 4D simulations covering civil, structural, MEP, white space, and commissioning phases. * Work closely with BIM leads to ensure model integrity, classification, and work package breakdown aligned with WBS structures. * Drive best practice in digital planning across Data Centre projects. * Develop 4D planning templates and standard workflows for repeatable campus developments. * Support integration of 4D outputs with Power BI or reporting dashboards where required. **Show your expertise** ----------------------- **Essential** * Relevant degree in construction * At least 5\-10 years Data Centre experience * Certification in project management / schedule management Advanced proficiency in: * Synchro 4D * Primavera P6 * BIM platforms (Revit / Navisworks / IFC workflows) * Proven understanding of construction sequencing for CSA, MEP, and commissioning\-driven environments. * Experience linking schedules to 3D models and managing model\-based quantity take\-offs. * Strong understanding of WBS structures, coding logic, and programme governance. * Ability to interface effectively with site teams, BIM managers, planners, and senior project leadership. **Contact:** ------------ You want to be part of the Exyte team? We look forward to receiving your application! Please note that we only consider applications submitted through our application portal. Applications sent via email will not be considered due to data protection regulations.

title: FP&A Analyst
description: A top\-tier global investment manager is hiring an FP\&A Analyst to join its growing Dublin Finance team. This role will make a global impact immediately Commercial, forward\-looking and high\-visibility. This is a role for a sharp analyst who wants direct involvement in decision\-making at senior level, not a back\-office reporting function. **WHY THIS ROLE STANDS OUT** 🟣 Newly created team in Dublin \- real scope to shape and influence from day one 🔵 Direct exposure to global FP\&A teams and senior leadership 🟣 Blend of commercial FP\&A and technical finance in a high\-performance environment 🔵 Cross\-functional remit spanning Risk, Strategy and HR on firm\-wide projects 🟣 Fast\-paced, meritocratic culture where strong analysts get recognised **THE ROLE** 🟣 Own monthly financial dashboards and reporting for senior management 🔵 Analyse revenue, cost drivers and KPIs to sharpen forecasting accuracy 🟣 Partner with business leaders on budgeting and strategic planning 🔵 Build financial models to support product expansion and growth decisions 🟣 Support financial reporting as required across the broader Finance function **CANDIDATE PROFILE** 🟣 4 \- 8 years FP\&A experience \- financial services background preferred 🔵 Strong Excel; VBA/macros a plus 🟣 Clear communicator with the confidence to influence senior stakeholders 🔵 Comfortable operating at pace in a demanding, high\-pressure environment

title: FP&A Junior Analyst
description: **We’re looking for a FP\&A Junior Analyst (12\-month FTC)** Based in Dublin reporting to the FP\&A Manager, this role will support the Global Financial Planning \& Analysis (FP\&A) team. This is a fantastic opportunity for those who are keen on a business facing or commercial finance career within a growing multi\-national (re)insurance operation with a strong presence in Dublin. We are looking for a motivated and ambitious individual; keen to learn about business and acquire a broad range of analytical and interpersonal skills. **What you will do** * Ongoing analysis of the Hamilton financial results: * Assist in monitoring performance; highlighting trends and analysing causes of unexpected variances * Completion of the quarterly financial results presentation and analytics providing insight to senior management on variances to plan. * Reconcile General Ledger (SUN) \& Management reporting tool (TM1\) * Annual Planning and Ongoing Forecasting: * Contribute to the completion of the annual budget cycle and the ongoing incremental forecasting process * Liaise with management, underwriting and support teams to assist setting annual budgets * Provide support to the FP\&A team, cost centre owners and management for ad\-hoc projects, tasks and analysis * Assist in continuous improvement of the FP\&A function and preparing process documentation **What you require for the role** * A numerate individual with a relevant university degree seeking to obtain a professionally recognised accounting qualification (ACA, ACCA, CIMA or equivalent) * Excellent attention to detail * Proven ability to work to deadlines * A motivated self\-starter * Team player but able to work towards individual targets * Articulate with good verbal and written communication skills * Proficient Microsoft Office – in particular, Microsoft Excel \& PowerPoint * Prior experience with SUN/TM1(IBM Planning Analytics)/Power BI is desirable but not essential **What you can expect from us** Join us, and we will ask you to Be smart, Be sensible, Be open and, above all else, Be more—strive harder, reach further, never give up. These First Principles are the intellectual and moral compass of everyone at Hamilton. They shape the way we build our team, the way we work and the way we will prosper. We’re committed to creating an inclusive workspace where all employees thrive. We embrace all backgrounds, perspectives, experiences and talents – without discrimination. Whatever differences set you apart, whether visible or invisible, you will be welcome at Hamilton. This is a 12\-month FTC position with a competitive salary and an attractive package of benefits. **We’re writing the future of risk.** Hamilton Insurance Group, Ltd. (“Hamilton”) is a Bermuda\-headquartered company that underwrites specialty insurance and reinsurance risks on a global basis through its wholly owned subsidiaries. Hamilton leverages analytics and research to create underwriting and investment value for its clients and shareholders.

title: Facilities and Sustainability Manger
description: Facilities \& Sustainability Manager **Our people make the difference.** Here at PHX Ireland, we strive to be a place for everyone \- where each and every one of us can be our true, authentic selves, every single day. For us, individuality sparks new ideas, shapes new approaches and brings us powerful new perspectives. It's the heartbeat of our innovation, creativity, and success. Which is why, at PHX Ireland, we don't just embrace our differences \- we celebrate them. Across United Drug, McCabes Pharmacy, and TCP Homecare, in every location and every role, the way we work makes us who we are \- and that's something we are really proud of. Which is why we've summed up our ethos in our six PHX Ireland values; Customer focused, Quality driven, collaborative, ambitious, innovative, and inclusive. ***Bring your energy to our team, and make millions of lives better.*** **Why You'll Love Working With Us:** Family comes first: Life Stage Policies including maternity and paternity pay Perks you'll actually use: Generous in store discounts Pension Support: We contribute to your future We reward performance: annual bonus We've got your back: employee assistance programme and wellbeing initiatives **Belonging at Work** : Inclusive, welcoming team culture where you're valued for *you* . **What You'll Be Doing:** **Facilities Management** * Provide end\-to\-end facilities management oversight across a geographically dispersed, multi\-site portfolio including distribution centres, offices locations. * Own the operational performance of all hard and soft FM services, including building fabric, HVAC, electrical and mechanical systems, fire detection and suppression, access control, utilities, and grounds maintenance. * Develop, implement, and maintain planned preventative maintenance (PPM) and statutory inspection programmes to ensure asset reliability, safety, and compliance. * Lead and support the planning and delivery of CAPEX projects, including warehouse expansions, major refurbishments, plant and equipment upgrades, and critical infrastructure works, from business case development through procurement, delivery, commissioning, and handover. * Act as the accountable owner for facilities contracts, managing tendering, mobilisation, performance management, KPI/SLA governance, and continuous improvement of service providers. * Lead facilities budgeting, forecasting, and cost control, ensuring transparent cost models, robust financial governance, and delivery of value\-for\-money outcomes. * Identify and manage lifecycle replacement and capital investment programmes, including business case development, risk assessment, and post\-implementation review. * Ensure effective utilities management, including consumption monitoring, tariff review, anomaly investigation, and cost optimisation. * Maintain accurate asset registers, drawings, certifications, and compliance records, ensuring all facilities documentation is audit\-ready and aligned with internal procedures and Irish statutory requirements. * Support site openings, closures, refurbishments, and change initiatives, ensuring facilities readiness, compliance, and minimal operational disruption. * Collaborate closely with Operations, Security, Cleaning, IT, and H\&S teams to ensure facilities services support operational performance, resilience, and business continuity. **Sustainability \& ESG** * Develop, implement, and continuously refine the organisation's sustainability strategy, ensuring alignment with corporate ESG objectives, operational priorities, and regulatory expectations. * Lead energy, carbon, water, and waste reduction programmes across the estate, translating sustainability targets into practical, site\-level initiatives with measurable outcomes. * Own energy and environmental performance monitoring, including data collection, analysis, benchmarking, and reporting to support informed decision\-making and performance improvement. * Drive SEAI grant applications, audits, and compliance activities, ensuring projects are scoped, delivered, and evidenced in line with scheme requirements. * Support and deliver decarbonisation initiatives, including energy efficiency upgrades, electrification, renewable technologies, and low\-carbon infrastructure investments. * Work closely with Facilities, Operations, and Procurement teams to embed sustainability principles into capital projects, asset selection, and supplier engagement. * Track, analyse, and report sustainability KPIs, supporting internal management reporting, audits, and external ESG disclosures as required **Health, Safety \& Compliance** * Ensure full compliance with Health \& Safety legislation, fire safety, environmental regulations, and internal SOPs. * Act as a key stakeholder during audits, inspections, and regulatory engagements. * Manage risk assessments, incident follow\-ups, corrective actions, and continuous improvement initiatives. * Support emergency preparedness, business continuity, and site resilience planning. **What You'll Need to Succeed:** * Bachelor's degree in environmental science, Engineering, Facilities Management, or related field. * Proven experience in sustainability management and facilities operations, preferably in a warehouse or distribution environment. * Strong understanding of environmental regulations and compliance requirements. * Excellent project management skills, with the ability to prioritise tasks and meet deadlines. * Effective communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate across departments and influence stakeholders. * Analytical mindset with the ability to collect, analyse, and interpret data to drive decision\-making. * Demonstrated commitment to sustainability and a passion for driving positive environmental change. **Personal Attributes** * Highly motivated, self\-driven professional with a strong sense of ownership and accountability for outcomes. * A people\-focused individual who builds strong, trusted relationships across all levels of the organisation and with external partners. * An effective communicator and influencer, able to engage stakeholders, bring people on the journey, and drive behavioural change. * Proactive and solutions\-focused, with the ability to anticipate issues and take decisive action. * Highly organised with strong attention to detail, capable of managing multiple priorities in a fast\-paced, regulated environment. * Comfortable operating at both strategic and operational levels, translating strategy into practical delivery. * Committed to continuous improvement, collaboration, and developing high\-performing, engaged teams. **About United Drug** United Drug is Ireland's largest pharmaceutical distribution company and was originally founded by a group of pharmacists in Ballina, Mayo in 1948\. In 2022 United Drug became part of the PHOENIX group and is now part of Europe's leading healthcare provider in the sector. Employing more than 650 staff across operations in Dublin, Limerick and Mayo, we partner with more than 1,900 pharmacies, hospitals, clinics and primary care centres across Ireland. Our business spans across three business units; United Drug Wholesale, United Drug Distribution and United Drug Consumer \- all leaders in their respective categories. **About PHX Ireland \& PHOENIX Group** We are comprised of United Drug, Ireland's leading pharmaceutical wholesaler and distributor; McCabes Pharmacy, Ireland's leading community pharmacy chain; and TCP Homecare, innovators in clinical home nursing services and direct to patient delivery solutions. PHX Ireland Group employs over 1,800 people, operates 5 distribution centres and pharmacies nationwide, delivering over 85 million packs to Irish pharmacies and hospitals and serving 500,000 patients each year. PHX Ireland is part of the PHOENIX group, Europe's leading healthcare provider. Headquartered in Mannheim, Germany, the PHOENIX group brings together more than 48,000 employees from 29 countries, includes 223 pharmaceutical distribution sites, and more than 3200 owned pharmacies and 17000 partner pharmacies. ***We strive to foster an open and inclusive environment, embracing applications from individuals of all backgrounds. Our commitment lies in ensuring an accessible interview process for all candidates. If you require additional support or accommodations to attend an interview, we kindly encourage you to reach out to*** ***jobs@phxireland.ie***

title: Field Sales Associate - Leinster Region
description: **About Us** Great salespeople don't wait for opportunities \- they create them. We're looking for commission\-driven, target\-focused professionals who are ready to make their mark. Small business owners are at the heart of everything we do. Ireland's small businesses are the backbone of our economy, and they deserve a payments \& financial management platform that is built for them. That's exactly what SumUp delivers, and we're growing our field sales team to bring it to businesses in every corner of the country. **Role Description** We're looking for a driven and commercially sharp Field Sales Associate to join our growing Irish team. In this role, you'll be out in the field every day, building genuine relationships with local business owners and showing them how SumUp's suite of payment and financial management tools can transform the way they run their businesses. This role is available to those in the surrounding **Dublin area/Leinster region**, including \- **Swords, Drogheda, Meath North, and Wicklow.** Ireland's small business community is thriving, but it is also evolving fast. Consumers expect seamless \& flexible payment options. Business owners need more than just a card reader \- they need an integrated solution that helps them manage transactions, track their finances, and make smarter decisions in real time. SumUp gives them exactly that, and your job is to make sure they know it. From day one, you'll hit the ground running \- identifying opportunities, building your pipeline, and closing deals. You'll have a full product toolkit at your disposal and the support of a fast\-moving team behind you, but the drive to generate results from the get\-go needs to come from you. If you're energised by targets, thrive in a face\-to\-face sales environment, and want to play a meaningful role in empowering Irish businesses with the financial tools they truly deserve, we want to hear from you. **What You'll Do:** * Prospect and engage SMBs through cold calling, door\-to\-door visits, and local networking. * Generate and qualify leads, booking meetings and converting opportunities in the field. * Deliver face\-to\-face sales pitches and product demos to business owners. * Build a strong self\-sourced pipeline using cold outreach, referrals, and market knowledge. * Handle objections confidently and tailor solutions to each merchant’s needs. * Develop long\-term relationships to drive repeat business and referrals. **You'll Be a Great Fit If You...** * Have 2\+ years experience in b2b Field Sales or a similar outbound role \- ideally within hospitality, retail, or another point\-of\-sale environment. * Can hit the ground running from day one: generating your own opportunities, managing a pipeline, and closing deals without needing a long ramp\-up period. * Consistently exceed sales targets and take full ownership of your results. * Know the Irish market \- you understand how local businesses operate, what challenges they face, and how to build trust quickly within your community. * Have hands\-on experience with outbound lead generation, cold calling, and building a pipeline from scratch. * Can speak passionately and knowledgeably about the role payments technology plays in helping Irish businesses grow and compete. * Are self\-motivated, resilient, and energised by working independently in the field. **Why Join SumUp?** * Competitive compensation package, including base salary, mobility/incidentals allowances, and **uncapped commission** \- your earnings grow directly with your results. * Private Health Insurance with Vitality (including optical and dental) and Life Assurance cover with MetLife (2x salary). * SumUp Corporate Pension Scheme. * Individual development budget for conferences and external training. * Access to the SumUp Sabbatical, Employee Referral Programme, and Employee Assistance Programme for mental health. * Regular team events, office socials, breakfasts, and sports \- we work hard and celebrate together. * A genuine opportunity to help reshape the payments landscape for Irish small. businesses and be part of something that makes a real difference in local communities across the country. *SumUp is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer that proudly pursues and hires a diverse workforce. SumUp does not make hiring or employment decisions on the basis of race, colour, religion or religious belief, ethnic or national origin, nationality, sex, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability, age or any other basis protected by applicable laws or prohibited by company policy. SumUp also strives for a healthy and safe workplace and strictly prohibits harassment of any kind.* *SumUp will not accept unsolicited resumes from any source other than directly from a candidate.* *Sales, Customer Service, Field Sales Representative, Field Sales, Sales Representative, Sales Executive,**Self Employed, Sales Assistant, Account Manager, Account Executive, Sales Manager, Car Sales, Retail,**Door to Door* **Job Application Tip** We recognise that candidates feel they need to meet 100% of the job criteria in order to apply for a job. Please note that this is only a guide. If you don’t tick every box, it’s ok too because it means you have room to learn and develop your career at SumUp.

title: Financial Analyst
description: ***On behalf of an international law firm in Dublin city centre, we are looking to hire a Financial Analyst.*** This is an excellent opportunity for a new qualified accountant to move into a commercially focused role with strong exposure to *data analysis* and business partnering. Working closely with senior stakeholders across the business, you will play a key role in delivering insights that support strategic decision\-making. This position offers a strong blend of financial analysis, stakeholder interaction, and exposure to ongoing business performance. **Key Responsibilities:** * Analyse financial and operational data to identify trends and key business drivers * Develop and maintain robust Excel models to support reporting and forecasting * Support budgeting and forecasting processes * Partner with internal stakeholders across multiple departments * Assist in the preparation of management reports and presentations **Qualifications:** * Recently qualified ACA / ACCA (or equivalent) with 0\-2 years PQE * Big 4 trained with strong Excel skills (comfortable working with large datasets, building models, etc.) * A genuine interest in data analysis and deriving insights from numbers * Excellent communication skills, with the ability to engage with and present to non\-finance stakeholders * Experience with Power BI, Python or similar tools is advantageous, but not essential This is an excellent opportunity for a newly qualified accountant to step into a commercially focused role, gaining valuable exposure to senior stakeholders while developing strong analytical and business partnering skills. For more information, apply within or contact cdignam@alliedtalent.ie

title: Financial Planning Associate
description: **The Company** A well\-established, client\-focused wealth management firm based in Dublin, working with private clients to deliver long\-term financial planning across investments, retirement, and family wealth. **The Rol** e Working as part of a close\-knit team, you'll partner with Private Client Managers to build and present comprehensive, long\-term financial plans for high\-net\-worth clients. This is a client\-facing role with real responsibility from day one. * Building holistic financial plans and presenting recommendations to clients * Preparing written suitability reports in line with compliance standards * Managing and reviewing client portfolios on an ongoing basis * Staying current with financial services developments and regulation **The Person** * Strong technical financial planning knowledge * Familiarity with cashflow modelling software (Voyant or similar) * CRM experience * Full adherence to compliance standards * Competitive salary and bonus * Pension \& life cover * Flexible working * Maternity/paternity benefits * Exam and membership fees covered

title: Food and Beverage Assistant
description: **The Heritage** offers a sophisticated elegance with impeccable hospitality set in the wonderful Laois countryside. It comprises of a luxury world class Hotel and European Award Winning Spa. **The Heritage** also offers guests a number of on\-site activities such as our Health Club with Leisure Pool, Jacuzzi, Sauna and Steam rooms, 5km Walking Track and Cinema. We have an exciting opportunity for two experienced **Food and Beverage Assistants** to join our team! The successful candidates will require **full\-time** flexibility and be available to work early, late, weekends, bank holidays. The successful candidate must be able to fulfill full\-time hours year round. **Key Responsibilities:** * To greet all guests with a smile, eye contact and a greeting if appropriate * To maintain your station in a clean and tidy condition at all times * To carry\-out mise en place for service, i.e., setting tables, stocking stations, preparing tea / coffee etc. * To correctly clear, polish and store crockery, cutlery and glassware to minimise breakages and loss, while maintaining standards * To correctly take customer orders maximising and promoting sales by recommending products where appropriate * To serve all our guests in a friendly, efficient and professional manner * To ensure cleanliness levels are maintained to a high standard in the bar and restaurant * To deal with any customer complaints in a professional and efficient manner, ensuring guest satisfaction at all times * To have knowledge of wines, spirits and the menu items * To handle guest's complaints discretely, escalating to management where required * Any other duties as reasonably required by management **Role Requirements:** * A professional and friendly approach to customers and colleagues * Focused on providing excellent customer service * Ideally, 1 year experience in a high\-volume establishment, however full training will be provided * Team player and have the ability to multitask * Excellent attention to detail * Flexibility to work early, late, weekends, bank holidays **Why Work for The Heritage, Part of FBD Hotels:** * Discounted rates in other FBD properties * Health Club membership * Referral scheme * Learning and development opportunities * Employee Assistance Programme * Strong team focus and team atmosphere * Meals provided on duty If you believe that this is the next role for you, please send your CV for consideration

title: Forward Deployed Engineer, Google Cloud Consulting
description: **Minimum qualifications:** * Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Computer Science, a related field, or equivalent practical experience. * 5 years of experience with software development using Python or similar coding languages. * Experience taking AI solutions from conception to launch and architecting AI systems on cloud platforms (e.g., Google Cloud Platform (GCP)). * Experience building pipelines for structured and unstructured data using both vector databases and retrieval augmented generation like architectures to power enterprise AI solutions. * Experience leading technical discovery sessions with customers. * Experience architecting AI systems on cloud platforms (e.g., GCP). **Preferred qualifications:** * Master’s degree or PhD in AI, Computer Science, or a related technical field. * Experience implementing multi\-agent systems using frameworks (e.g., LangGraph, CrewAI, Agent Development Kit (ADK)) and complex patterns (e.g., ReAct, self\-reflection, hierarchical delegation). * Experience in a post\-sales or technical consulting delivery function. * Knowledge of large language model native metrics (e.g., tokens/sec, cost\-per\-request) and techniques for optimizing state management and granular tracing. **About The Job** As a Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE) in the Google Cloud Consulting organization, you will be an embedded builder who bridges the gap between frontier AI products and production\-grade reality within customers. Unlike traditional advisory roles, you will function as an "innovator\-builder," moving beyond high\-level architecture to code, debug, and jointly ship bespoke agentic solutions directly within the customer’s environment. This role is designed for high\-agency engineers with a founder’s mindset. You will address blockers to production including solving the integration complexities, data readiness issues, and state\-management issues that prevent AI from reaching enterprise\-grade maturity. By embedding with accounts, you will serve a dual purpose: providing "white glove" deployment of complex AI systems and acting as a critical feedback loop, transforming real\-world field insights into Google Cloud’s future product roadmap. It's an exciting time to join Google Cloud’s Go\-To\-Market team, leading the AI revolution for businesses worldwide. You’ll excel by leveraging Google's brand credibility—a legacy built on inventing foundational technologies and proven at scale. We’ll provide you with the world's most advanced AI portfolio, including frontier Gemini models, and the complete Vertex AI platform, helping you to solve business problems. We’re a collaborative culture providing direct access to DeepMind's engineering and research minds, empowering you to solve customer challenges. Join us to be the catalyst for our mission, drive customer success, and define the new cloud era—the market is yours. Individual pay is determined by factors including job\-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Ireland: €122000 \- €126000 (EUR) \+ 15% bonus target \+ equity \+ benefits **Responsibilities** Learn more about benefits at Google . * Serve as a developer for complex AI applications, transitioning from rapid prototypes to production\-grade agentic workflows (e.g., multi\-agent systems, model context protocol (MCP) servers) that drive measurable Return on Investment (ROI). * Architect and code the "connective tissue" between Google’s AI products and customer's live infrastructure, including APIs, legacy data silos, and security perimeters as part of an expert team. * Build high\-performance evaluation pipelines and observability frameworks to ensure agentic systems meet requirements for accuracy, safety, and latency. * Identify repeatable field patterns and friction points in Google’s AI stack, converting them into reusable modules or formal product feature requests for the Engineering teams. * Co\-build with pre\-sales and product teams to instill Google\-grade development best practices, ensuring long\-term project success and high end\-user adoption. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

title: Founding AE
description: **Founding AE – Hypergrowth SaaS Start\-up** Dublin \| 5 days on\-site \| Equity included We’re partnering with one of the fastest\-growing SaaS start\-up companies in Dublin right now. They're growing rapidly and are now looking to hire a Founding AE who wants to be part of something that is genuinely going to the moon. This isn’t a role for someone who wants to sit in a big, structured sales org. It’s for someone who wants autonomy, ownership, and the chance to be part of a company that could become a real success story in the Dublin tech scene over the next few years. **The role** * Own the full sales cycle from discovery through to close * Sell into mid\-market customers across multiple industries * Work closely with the founders and leadership team * Help shape the sales process as the company scales * Take full ownership of pipeline and performance * Operate in a fast\-moving, high\-autonomy environment **What they’re looking for (essential)** * 3–5 years of closing experience in a SaaS sales role * Currently or previously carrying a **7\-figure annual quota** * Ideally Start up/Scale up experience * Can commit to 5 days on site in Dublin City Centre * Someone who is comfortable working in a fast\-growth start\-up * Self\-sufficient, driven, and comfortable with ownership * High energy, ambitious, and wants to be part of a company that’s going places **Why this role stands out** * Join early in what is expected to be a **hypergrowth journey** * Real autonomy – not a heavily micromanaged environment * **Equity included** as part of the package * Opportunity to grow with the company as it scales * Be part of a company that is aiming to become one of the standout start\-ups in Dublin Contact james.slein@adamantinehq.com for more information.

title: Full Stack Engineer C#
description: **Full Stack Engineer C\#** **Permanent, Dublin, Onsite** **About the Company** My client is a global financial firm with a reputation for innovation and technology\-driven solutions. They operate in a fast\-paced environment where quick thinking and technical expertise are critical to success. They are recognised for an entrepreneurial culture and data\-driven approach. Their teams design and implement advanced trading strategies while developing proprietary technology that powers high\-performance, low\-latency trading across global markets. If you're the kind of engineer who sees patterns where others see noise, who plays games not just for fun but to win, and who genuinely believes that rigorous thinking applied to hard problems is its own reward, you'll fit right in. **About the Position** Due to outstanding growth, my client is looking to hire exceptional Full Stack Developers with strong proficiency in C\#/.NET to join a fast\-paced, iterative development team delivering business\-critical systems. Ideal candidates will have a deep understanding of end\-to\-end system impact and long term system stability, as well as strong communication skills. Key indicators for success will be the ability to balance technical excellence, ownership of their work and the ability to collaborate closely with both technical and business stakeholders. **Key Responsibilities** • Design, build, and maintain robust full\-stack applications using C\#/.NET and related technologies. • Operate in an iterative development environment using lightweight specifications and fast feedback loops. • Collaborate closely with traders, business users, support teams, and fellow developers. • Contribute to system and application architecture with a focus on scalability, resilience, and performance. • Take ownership of systems across the full lifecycle, from requirements and design through to production support. • Support production environments at 2nd level, ensuring system stability and prioritising operational improvements. • Participate actively in design discussions, reviews, and knowledge\-sharing sessions. • Apply pragmatic decision\-making guided by speed, capacity, stability, and risk. **Experience/Requirements** • Proficiency in C\#/.NET and WPF framework • Experience in at least one additional programming language (e.g. Python or JavaScript) • Solid understanding of software design, debugging, and problem solving • Experience working with distributed systems and business\-critical applications • Familiarity with modern development tools and practices (e.g. Git\-based workflows, CI/CD) • Experience working across the full software development lifecycle, including production support • Strong written and verbal communication skills, particularly in collaborative, fast\-moving environments **Desirable** • Experience in financial, trading, or time\-sensitive environments • Exposure to cloud platforms, containerisation, or system monitoring • Experience with databases and writing complex queries • Experience in software performance tuning for high throughput or low latency • Experience contributing to documentation, mentoring, or engineering standards **Remuneration Package** Along with a market leading salary and benefits package, you will enjoy an innovative environment, have access to on\-site facilities. **Contact** Please contact Charlie Bigger on 01 5927867 or email or simply click the apply button. To view all live jobs with Brightwater and market insights, please visit our website: www.brightwater.ie

title: General Operative
description: Nature’s Best Ltd is a leading Irish manufacturer of fresh salads and chilled convenience foods, based in Drogheda, Co. Louth. As a long\-established, family\-run business, we are committed to producing high\-quality products while fostering a supportive and team\-oriented workplace. **The Role** We are currently seeking **General Operatives** to join our busy production facility. This role is ideal for individuals who are reliable, hardworking, and looking to build a long\-term career in the food manufacturing industry. You will be working as part of a team to ensure our products are produced, packed, and handled to the highest standards of quality, hygiene, and food safety. **Key Responsibilities** * Operate machinery and equipment safely and efficiently * Assist in the preparation, packing, and labelling of products * Carry out quality checks to ensure products meet company standards * Maintain a clean and hygienic work environment in line with food safety regulations * Follow all company procedures relating to health \& safety and hygiene * Work collaboratively with team members and supervisors to meet daily production targets **What We Offer** * Full\-time, permanent contract with long\-term stability * Competitive starting rate of €14\.15 per hour, with opportunities for pay progression * Full training provided – no previous experience required * Opportunities for career progression within the company * Supportive team environment in a fast\-paced and growing industry **Requirements** * Flexibility to work a variety of shifts * Strong work ethic with good reliability and timekeeping * Ability to work effectively as part of a team * Good attention to detail and commitment to quality * Willingness to learn and follow procedures * Previous experience in food production or manufacturing is an advantage, but not essential **How to Apply** If you are looking for a secure, full\-time role with opportunities to grow and develop your career, we would love to hear from you. Please apply with your CV and a brief note outlining your interest in the role. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: From €14\.15 per hour Benefits: * On\-site parking Work Location: In person

title: Geographic Information System Intern
description: **GIS \& Planning Intern** **About the Role** We are looking for a motivated GIS \& Planning Intern to support EV infrastructure planning and network expansion activities across Ireland and the UK. The role will involve assisting with GIS analysis, spatial data management, and site feasibility studies to support the growth of EV charging infrastructure. The intern will work closely with the Network Planning Team to analyse existing charging networks, identify opportunities for expansion, and contribute to data\-driven planning decisions. **Key Responsibilities** * Support analysis of existing EV charging infrastructure and network coverage. * Assist with EV infrastructure site feasibility assessments and network expansion studies. * Prepare maps, GIS visualisations, and analytical reports. * Help identify network gaps, demand opportunities, and spatial trends. * Assist in the management, maintenance, and analysis of spatial datasets. * Support the development and improvement of GIS workflows and planning methodologies. * Collaborate with planning, operations, and commercial teams on infrastructure planning projects. * Contribute to research and data collection activities to support strategic planning initiatives. **What We Are Looking For** * Recently completed, a degree in GIS, Geography, Urban Planning, Transport Planning, or a related field. * Understanding and hands\-on experience of GIS concepts, spatial analysis, and mapping techniques. * Experience using ArcGIS Pro or QGIS through coursework, projects, internships, or personal learning. * Interest in EV infrastructure, sustainable mobility, transport systems, and spatial planning. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills with attention to detail. * Ability to work with datasets and interpret spatial information. * Good communication and organisational skills. * Familiarity with Python, SQL, spatial analytics, or geospatial data processing would be an advantage but is not essential. **Project Work / Portfolio** Candidates are encouraged to share coursework, university projects, internships, research, or personal projects demonstrating: * GIS analysis and mapping * Spatial analysis and visualisation * Urban or transport planning studies * Infrastructure or accessibility assessments * Data analysis and problem\-solving using GIS methodologies **What You’ll Gain** * Practical experience in EV infrastructure planning and network expansion. * Exposure to real\-world GIS, planning, and spatial analytics projects. * Experience working across planning, operations, and commercial teams. * Development of technical, analytical, and professional skills. * Insight into the rapidly growing EV and sustainable transport sector. **Location** Maynooth **Duration** Internship (Flexible depending on availability) Paid Internship \- €14\.15 per hour

title: Global Sports Trader
description: * Dublin, Ireland * Full time 40 hours * Permanent ### **Role Purpose** As a Global Sports Trader, you will have the opportunity to channel your innovative and inquisitive attitude into serving up an exciting, bespoke product to Flutter customers across some of the biggest sporting events of the year. This role focuses on our cutting\-edge Tennis product and will provide the successful candidate the opportunity to work on some of the most prestigious events on the Tennis calendar including Wimbledon, Roland Garros, the Australian Open and the US Open amongst others. Our team spread across Dublin and Melbourne bring together a wealth of experiences and perspectives, fuelling innovative and creative solutions 24/7 365 days a year to ensure our brands and customers have the most exciting Tennis product available across the globe ### **Accountabilities \& Responsibilities** * Ideation, management, and compilation of a range of compelling markets for Flutter customers across 100,000\+ Tennis events per year * Working closely with cross\-functional teams, sharing insights and fostering a culture where every perspective is valued. * Interacting confidently with a broad range of internal and external partners * Maintaining a keen eye for detail in creating and managing attractive betting opportunities. * Comfortable with ambiguity and capable of making quick decisions with limited information ### **What We’re Looking For** * Openness to learning and applying AI and emerging technologies. * Clear and thoughtful communication skills that help you connect with colleagues, partners and suppliers globally * A genuine focus on understanding customer needs combined with strong attention to detail. * Good understanding on Excel and similar software products. A flexible attitude toward working hours, including evenings, weekends, and bank holidays when needed. * While knowing about odds and probabilities is a plus, we offer continuous training to get you fully up to speed. * An interest in Tennis is a bonus, but we welcome candidates with a variety of interests and experiences. ### **Benefits** Finance \& Health: * Uncapped holiday allowance! * Private healthcare * Company Pension Plan, Life assurance, and income protection * Discretionary Annual bonus * Carers allowance * Share Save Scheme, discount vouchers, and volunteering days * Travel Save \& Bike to Work * €1,000 annual self\-development learning fund with access to thousands of Udemy courses ### **Lifestyle \& Wellbeing:** * Hybrid and flexible working * Parental, Non\-birthing parent and Adoption leave * Lean In Circles, Allyship coaching and DE\&I events throughout year * Onsite perks (e.g. onsite Gym, nail technician, hairdresser and barber) * €250 Health and Wellbeing Fund * Subsidised Canteen, Cafe’s and shop. * Social Events, Golf Society and Run Club. * Paid Volunteering Days * The opportunity to work abroad for 20 days per year! ### **Measure of Success** * Pursues continuous professional development, actively seeking new learning opportunities and adapting to market changes. * Plays an active role in planning and analysing events, ensuring that a variety of perspectives inform decisions. * Works collaboratively with colleagues across teams, openly sharing insights and learning together. * Makes confident decisions that add new value, always acting with honesty and fairness. * Seeks and takes advantage of opportunities as they arise, even when they fall outside standard procedures. * Regularly reviews performance feedback at all levels to foster ongoing improvement. ### **About Division/Function** Global Sports \& Trading (GST) is an internal service provider within Flutter tasked with providing the world’s most compelling sports betting products to our brands across the Globe as they shape the future of sports betting. Customer Commitment We provide Flutter brands with innovative Sportsbook and Trading services, giving them simple access to exactly what they need \- when they need it. We’re a trusted partner, allowing brands to focus on delivering unforgettable experiences to their customers. Our unique culture encourages innovative solutions to big customer problems, by teams not afraid to take risks. We're collectively committed to building a culture of inclusion, innovation, personal growth and leadership excellence. It’s an incredibly exciting time to join the us as we are doubling down on our ambition to transform global betting and gaming. Using our collective power, the Flutter Edge, we will continue to disrupt our sector and to create a better future for our customers, colleagues and communities. ### **About Flutter** Flutter is the world’s leading online sports betting and iGaming operator, with a market leading position in the US and across the world. Our ambition is to demonstrate our significant scale and our challenger mindset to change our industry for the better. By Changing the Game, we believe we can deliver long\-term growth while promoting a positive, sustainable future for all our partners. We are well\-placed to do so through the distinctive, global advantages of the Flutter Edge, which gives our brands access to group\-wide benefits to stay ahead of the competition, as well as our clear vision for sustainability through our Positive Impact Plan. Flutter operates a diverse portfolio of leading online sports betting and iGaming brands including FanDuel, Sky Betting \& Gaming, Sportsbet, PokerStars, Paddy Power, Sisal, Tombola, Betfair, MaxBet, Junglee Games and Adjarabet. **We’re working to be an inclusive employer, and we encourage people from all backgrounds, ways of thinking and working to apply. Everyone brings different perspectives and experiences; you don't have to meet all the requirements listed to apply for this role. Everyone brings different perspectives and experiences; you don't have to meet all the requirements listed to apply for this role. title: Goods In Administrator
description: **PURPOSE** The purpose of this work instruction is to outline the responsibilities of Goods in Administrators for managing goods\-in activities in the warehouse, in accordance with SOP\-33\-003 Goods in Procedure (Greenogue 2\). This work instruction applies to all goods received across Uniphar Supply Chain and Retail facilities using SAP EWM. It outlines how ambient, controlled drugs and refrigerated deliveries are overseen by Goods in Administrators. **Goods In Administrators are responsible for:** * Providing delivery slots to suppliers/ MAHs. * Ensuring that SF\-33\-009 Goods\-In Log (Greenogue 2\) is complete. * Ensuring that the temperature trace is provided by the driver prior to the delivery being unloaded. * Informing warehouse operatives of the arrival or expected arrival of a fridge order. * Advising drivers what bay to go to on arrival. * Verifying delivery of stock against paperwork (ICG) and compare ICG with the packing list. * Recording any discrepancies or issues with a delivery as a Goods\-In Non\-Conformance (GINC) and notifying the supplier/Marketing Authorization Holder (MAH). **Uniphar – Who we are** With a workforce of more than 3000 employees spread across Ireland, United Kingdom, Mainland Europe, MENA, and the USA, Uniphar is a trusted global partner to pharma and MedTech manufacturers, working to improve patient access to medicines around the world. Uniphar provides outsourced and specialized services to its clients, leveraging the strong relationships with 200\+ of the world’s best known pharmaco\-medical manufacturers across multiple geographies, enabled by our cutting\-edge digital technology and our highly expert teams. Uniphar is organised into three key divisions: Supply Chain Retail \& On Demand, Uniphar Med Tech and Uniphar Pharma. **Supply Chain \& Retail** Supply Chain Services provides both pre\-wholesale and wholesale distribution of pharmaceutical, healthcare and animal health products to pharmacies and hospital nationwide. The business supports a diverse customer base through the provision of strong service levels coupled with innovative commercial initiatives. In addition, the business provides services and supports that help independent community pharmacy to compete more effectively in an increasingly difficult environment and runs a network of Uniphar\-owned pharmacies under the Life, Allcare, McCauleys and Hickeys symbol brands. **Pharma – On Demand** The On\-Demand business is a specialist medical supply business that sources and distributes pharmaceuticals, medical equipment, and consumable supplies to healthcare professionals in over 160 countries worldwide. We have offices in Ireland, UK, Europe, USA and New Zealand. The company’s main divisions encompass everything from International Sales through Sales to NGO’s and Charities, Clinical Trials Supply, Imported (Unlicensed Medicines), Sexual Health Supplies, and the storage and distribution of medical products and devices for third parties**.** Click here to find out more: About Us \- Who we are \| Uniphar **Culture at Uniphar** Uniphar is a combination of many businesses each with its own identity, culture, values, and ways of working. Our overarching culture narrative is inclusive of each of these stores. Uniphar lives and breathes four key pillars which unpin and support our culture. We have a people first approach \- we do the right thing. We support entrepreneurial spirit where adaptability, commitment \& resilience is embodied in our way of working. We have a Common Purpose that connects our diversified businesses and people. Finally, Trust is at the heart of how we operate. Everything Uniphar does is enabled by our people and as we continue to grow domestically and internationally, we become more diverse. This diversity fuels our business and culture. **Sustainability** Sustainability is at the core of what we do and is deeply embedded in our business strategy. We want to contribute positively to the people and the world around us. Running our business in a sustainable way ensures that Uniphar will continue to prosper in the long\-term. We are focused on ensuring that each of the five pillars of our sustainability strategy are a fundamental part of our decision\-making process.

We are looking for a Graduate Environmental Health \& Safety Advisor to join our Ireland Field Service Operations team on a **24 month fixed‑term contract** , supporting a major industrial equipment installation and commissioning project in a controlled manufacturing environment *.* **What you will do:** This role is responsible for ensuring that installation and start‑up activities are carried out safely and in full compliance with corporate safety standards, regulatory requirements, and project‑specific controls. The Graduate Environmental Health \& Safety Advisor will work closely with project teams, contractors, and Environmental, Health \& Safety (EHS) partners to promote a strong safety culture and reduce risk throughout the project lifecycle. This position requires a visible, hands‑on safety presence at our customer site in **Leixlip, Co. Kildare,** and the ability to operate effectively in a fast‑paced, high‑risk project environment. **Role Responsibilities** * Carry out daily site safety walks and follow up on observations and permit‑to‑work activities (e.g. LOTO, confined spaces, working at heights) * Monitor compliance with safety procedures, PPE requirements, and site rules * Support incident and near‑miss investigations and corrective actions * Deliver toolbox talks and safety briefings as required * Prepare weekly and monthly safety reports and track safety KPIs * Support project readiness reviews, key installation proactive approach with a visible on‑site presence **Why Join This Project?** * Be part of a large‑scale, high‑visibility industrial project with strong safety leadership and clear standards * Gain hands‑on experience supporting complex equipment installation and commissioning activities * Work in a collaborative, on‑site environment alongside experienced project, engineering, and EHS professionals * Opportunity to make a real impact on safety performance and culture **Additional Information** **Time Type:** Full time **Employee Type:** Fixed Term (Fixed Term) **Travel:** No **Relocation Eligible:** No The salary offered to a selected candidate will be based on multiple factors including location, hire grade, job\-related knowledge, skills, experience, and with For all sales roles, the posted salary range is the Target Total Cash (TTC) range for the role, which is the sum of base salary and target bonus amount at 100% goal achievement.
